title: Grand and ashy ice cave
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Katla Ice Caves are the only year round ice cave in Iceland. If you want to visit an ice cave in summer season this is your option. They change every year but most of the time they are grand and very impressive. The Katla Ice Caves are more black than others since the dust from Katla volcano has colored the ice."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note: the meeting point for Arctic Adventures Ice Cave Tours is on the parking lot behind the IceWear store in Vik. This specfic location on the map is near the current ice cave entrance."}]}]}

title: A beautiful and relaxing lagoon
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Sky Lagoon is a fairly new spa in Reykjavik. Built next to the ocean the best time to visit is sunset and see the sun set into to ocean. Don't miss out on their amazing sauna."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I also recommend doing the 7-Step Ritual included in the Pure Entrance pass. The ritual includes:"}]},{"type":"orderedList","content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Main pool (great sunset views)"}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Cold plunge tub"}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Finnish sauna (great sunset views)"}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Cool mist shower"}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Exfoliating scrub"}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Steam room"}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Shower"}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may also order drinks while in the main pool and/or grab something to eat at their restaurant or cafe after your visit."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reservations are recommended but not required - just know that this is a very popular spot and even during off-peak travel season, they may be fully booked."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The changing rooms are separated by gender and do offer individual shower stalls with a door for privacy."}]}]}

title: Northern Lights Tour
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you want to maximise your chances of seeing the incredible northern lights, then a tour is a good idea. It can take some skills to get to the right area at the right time to spot them. So going with an experienced guide will increase your chances of seeing the lights."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I recommend to join the small group tour with Arctic Adventures. They have great guides and in case you don't see the lights, you can come back for free the day after. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Another really fun option is to do the lava tunnel & northern lights combo. The lava tunnel is massive and makes for an really fun adventure, even if the lights don't come out."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Remember to always dress warm and enjoy the show!"}]}]}

title: Dive between two tectonic plates
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Silfra has some of the clearest water in the world. It's a crack between the Eurasian and North Atlantic tectonic plates. The water is melted glacier ice, filtered through lava rock which makes it extremely clear. Here you can go snorkeling or diving with a visibility of over 100m. It feels like flying."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The water is cold, but you'll be safe in a dry suit provided by the tour company. You'll be in the water for about 45 min and this is really an incredible experience."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Plan to park at Þingvellir Parking P5 and walk to where all of the dive vans are parked."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Dress warmly!"}]}]}

title: Beautiful Volcanic Island
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I can’t recommend Vestmannaeyjar enough. It’s such a special place — small but full of charm and adventure. The ferry ride over is already part of the experience: it’s super scenic, and if the weather’s good, you’ll get amazing views of the cliffs and islands along the way."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Once you arrive, the town itself is cozy and welcoming, with colorful houses and a calm, friendly vibe. It’s the kind of place where you instantly feel at ease."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The hiking is seriously underrated here — you get epic views without needing to climb for hours. You can hike up the volcano that erupted in the 1970s, or explore the dramatic cliffs with the ocean below you. And if you love puffins (who doesn’t?), this is one of the best places to see them up close during the summer. There are thousands of them nesting in the cliffs!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"And of course, you can’t leave without seeing "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Elephant Rock"},{"type":"text","text":" — it really does look like a giant stone elephant rising from the sea."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you're up for a unique, nature-filled day or weekend trip with a bit of everything, Vestmannaeyjar is 100% worth it. I always leave wanting to go back."}]}]}

title: 4x4 Parking Geothermal area
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Kerlingarfjöll is looks like visiting planet March. The road here is very bumpy so you need a good 4x4 to reach it. It's rather easy to get to the mountain resort, but the last way up to the geothermal area is very steep. If you manage to get there, there's photo opportunities everywhere and as this landscape is something else."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Information about self-guided hikes can be found at the Highland Base reception. But, for planning purposes, visit this "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://highlandbase.is/adventures/hiking/on-your-own","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":"text-primary underline"}}],"text":"site"},{"type":"text","text":"."}]}]}

title: Vestmannaeyjar's best hike
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The best hike of Vestmannaeyjar. Perfect to do in the evening and see the sun set into the ocean. You'll get a great view over the harbour and in summer there's lots of puffins up here."}]}]}

title: Scenic glacier lagoon retreat in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is one of my favourite hotels in Iceland and I can highly recommend to stay here. Everything about this hotel os so beautiful. The views over the glaceisr, the hot tubs, the cozy sauna and the interior design. On top of that the food is great and they have one of the best breakfast buffets of Iceland. Oh and let's not forget that this is a perfect spot to watch northern lights!"}]}]}

title: Stokksnes Lupine Field
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is probably my favourite lupine spot in all of Iceland. Follow the path from the parking lot and you'll see some water on your left. There's a tiny island you can walk out to and there's this small lupine field. It has both pink and purple flowers and perfect to shoot with the Stokksnes mountain range in the background. Best light is at sunrise if you want to catch the frist rays of sun in the morning."}]}]}

title: River Crossing Waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Bláfjallafoss, also known as the Blue Mountains, is a stunning mountain range in southwest Iceland. They are mainly famous for this specific river crossing, where the road F232 crosses a river in front of a waterfall."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is in the highlands so you need a 4x4. Always check the depth of the water before crossing, it can change from day to day. The crossing is usually relatively easy and can be done on most 4x4s."}]}]}

title: The best puffin spot of Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is the best puffin spot of Iceland. This small peninsula is crowded by puffins and other sea birds. A small pathway has been built so you can walk just next to their nests and get really close to these incredible birds. Best time to see them is at sunset when they come back to land after spending the day fishing in the ocean."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Puffins arrive in Iceland mid-April and leave before the end of August."}]}]}

title: A traditional Viking dinner
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Want to eat a traditional Viking dinner in a Viking house? Ingolfsskáli is serving fine dining Viking food that is absolutely delicious. This restaurant is built in the traditional way with a grass roof and before dinner you have the option to try archery and axe-throwing. The perfect way to get into Viking mood and start your Iceland trip. Oh and don't forget to try the mead :)"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reservations required."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vegetarian options available."}]}]}

title: Laki Moss Road
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Laki road has some incredible moss sections where you drive through vibrant green landscapes. Best is to time it after a heavy rainfall, then the moss is very green"}]}]}

title: A waterfilled volcanic crater
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Bláhylur is a waterfilled volcanic crater. You can drive up to the lower part of the crater and park there. If you want the best view, hike up the steep hill. It is further than it looks, expect 20-30 minutes to get to the top. From here you'll have a great view over the crater and this is also the best photo spot. Bláhylur is great for droning."}]}]}

title: Yellow River Braid
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is the famous yellow river braid on the south coast of Iceland. It's easy to get to but please do respect the private land of farmers and be careful where you launch your drone from."}]}]}

title: Pretty waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"When driving into the highlands you pass this waterfall. It's a parking lot stop and very accessible."}]}]}

title: Hidden hot spring waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This hot spring is very new and one of my absolute favorite ones in Iceland. Situated just outside of Akureyri it's easy to get to but it's still very hidden. The water comes from a river and before the cliffs is a pool you can sit in. Then the water drops straight down into the ocean. It's really an epic waterfall. It's best in summer as the water can be a bit colder in winter."}]}]}

title: Whale watching from Reykjavik
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Did you know that you can see whales directly outside of Reykjavik? The bay has a lot of wildlife and high chances of seeing whales. The most common are Humpback and Minke whales. Dolphins are also very common and if you're lucky you'll spot Orcas as well."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The boat departs from downtown Reykjavik, so just hop on. If you go for the big boat, there's a cafe on board, I highly recommend the waffles. It's really cozy to be able to sit inside to stay warm and once the whales come, you go outside to see them."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"In summer time you can also "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://adventures.is/iceland/day-tours/whale-watching/rib-boat-whale-watching-from-reykjavik/?utm_source=asa&utm_medium=affiliate&utm_campaign=arctic_partnership","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":"text-title underline"}}],"text":"join the speedboat"},{"type":"text","text":". It's smaller and can go closer to the small islands and let you see puffins (May-August)."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The whale tour is a great family activity and kids of all ages are welcome to join!"}]}]}

title: Steep hike with amazing views
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Landmannalaugar is a hiking paradise and this is one of my favourite hikes in Iceland. Start by going up Bláhnjúkur, it's quite steep and great views from the top. Then follow the trail down the backside and into the valley. When the trail turns right and hugs the mountain, you can go out on a ridge and find this photo location. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Then follow the trail back to Landmannalaugar. It goes through a lava field which need a bit of balance to navigate. Finish the day in the hot spring :)"}]}]}

title: The canyon in "I'll Show You" by Justin Bieber
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This beautiful canyon got famous after Justin Bieber featured it in his music video from Iceland. It's well worth a visit. You can hike along the ridge on the right hand side but walking down below alongside the river is prohibited."}]}]}

title: A cute little hot spring
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a very cute hot spring with small changing rooms and understated surroundings. It has gotten very popular lately and can be crowded in high season."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It's a wonderful stop to make on your way to the South Coast from Hotel Geysir and is warm enough to enjoy year round!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Credit card payments are taken at the entrance hut."}]}]}

title: Hidden gem in Icelandic Highland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tjarnargígur Crater Park is a hidden gem in the Icelandic Highland, formed during the 1783-1784 Skaftáreldar eruption. This picturesque crater boasts a stunning turquoise pond at its center, surrounded by lava fields and moss-covered terrain. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You need a 4x4 to get here and there's a few river crossings along the way in."}]}]}

title: Glacier Hike in Skaftafell
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"One of my favorite glacier hikes in Iceland is the "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Glacier Explorer"},{"type":"text","text":" at Falljökull. The walk up to the glacier is already beautiful, and once you put on crampons and step onto the ice, it feels like another world. You get to explore blue ice formations, crevasses, and take in amazing views of the mountains all around."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If conditions are good, the guide even takes you into the icefall – where the glacier tumbles down the mountain toward the ocean. Standing there, surrounded by ancient ice, is such a powerful experience. It’s the perfect mix of adventure and awe, and one of those tours I always recommend."}]}]}

title: A hot spring cave
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This hot spring is super cool but sadly not that hot. It's about 35 degrees so all fine in summer but too cold for winter baths. You need to climb into it using a rope but once in it's an incredible place to swim around in."}]}]}

title: One of Iceland's biggest waterfalls
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Skogafoss is probably the most impressive waterfall of Iceland. It's 60m high and 20m wide with lots of water. It's a very easy accessible waterfall with parking lot just 200m away."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a very popular stop so expect crowds."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you can make it, head up the endless stairway, all the way to the top. This is the beginning of the Fimmvörðuháls trail - a 22 km trail that takes you between glaciers and into Þórsmörk. You don't need to do the entire trail though. If your itinerary allows, spend some time walking along \"waterfall way\" before turning back around."}]}]}

title: A river hot spring
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reykjadalur is a hot river about 1h outside of Reykjavik. It's perfect for a hike and a swim. The hike takes about 1h one way and goes up a scenic valley. When you get to the swim spot, feel the water before you get in as it's warmer the higher you go. Find your ideal temperature and enjoy!\nThere's a cafe at the start of the trial if you are hungry or need a bathroom. After the hike, check out Ingolfskali Viking restaurant, it's close by and have really nice Viking food!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The hike to the spring is mostly uphill along a gravel path but in the winter, the path is covered in snow and can be difficult to follow."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Once at the spring, there are no private changing facilities. Instead there are dividing walls that provide some privacy from those hiking and soaking. Be prepared to get naked with the wind blowing at your back!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Pay for parking at the cafe via EasyPark app or at the kiosk next to the cafe doors. NOTE: when using the kiosk, pay for parking just before you leave."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For those physically unable to do the hike, the cafe is a great, cozy hangout with coffee, snacks, and a fireplace."}]}]}

title: The Black Church of Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The famous black church of Iceland. There are a few more black churches but this is definitely the best looking one and with nice mountains in the backdrop."}]}]}

title: Campsite
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Highlands Base is a brand new hotel in Kerlingarfjöll. It's the perfect basecamp when exploring the highlands. They have a great restaurant and a spa with stunning views. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The hotel has something for everyone. There's luxury cabins with panorama views, regular hotel rooms and a campsite. If you're travelling through the Highlands, this is a must stop."}]}]}

title: Highlands day tour
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Going into Landmannalaugar is one of the most unique drives you can do in Iceland. Along the way are water filled craters, lava fields and epic waterfalls. After you cross the final river you make it to Landmannalaugar a sanctuary deep in the highlands. From here you can go on many hikes into the orange mountains or just soak in the hot spring. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A great way to explore all the best spots is to join a day trip from Reykjavik in a super jeep. Your guide will take you to the best locations and you don't need to worry about renting a 4x4 car."}]}]}

title: Kayak with icebergs in Jökulsárlon
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Kayaking among the icebergs is one of the best tours you can do in Iceland. It's pure magic to glide through the deep blue water and around these giants. These tours sell out way in advance so make sure to book early."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Available mid-May through September."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Must be 14 years or older."}]}]}

title: Viking Horseback Tours
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"One of the best riding tours of Iceland. The horses are great and before riding you'll get a proper lesson and then off you go. After riding you can stay for dinner and they also have a really nice cottage for the night to rent.\nI highly recommend to book the dinner as well, it's amazing!"}]}]}

title: Ice Climbing & Glacier Hikes
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Arctic Adventures at Skaftafell Base Camp is the ultimate hub for glacier exploration in Iceland. With certified glacier guides, top-notch gear like crampons and ice axes, and stunning landscapes to discover, it's the perfect place to embark on an unforgettable adventure. Whether you're a seasoned hiker or a first-time glacier explorer, Arctic Adventures has something for everyone. So gear up, get ready to step onto the shimmering ice, and let the glacier wonders of Skaftafell take your breath away!"}]}]}

title: Snowmobile on a glacier
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is the adventure for you if you want to go snowmobiling in Iceland. Drive up the Langjökull glacier and experience the speed of powerful snowmobiles. I recently got hooked on snowmobiles, it's so much fun and I can highly recommend this experience!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This tour doesn’t require previous snowmobile riding experience, and anyone over the age of 17 with a valid driver’s licence can operate the snowmobile. But, if there are kids over 8 in the group or non-drivers, they can ride as a passenger. Get ready to glide on a glacier!"}]}]}

title: Whale watching tours in Akureyri
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Sail ot from Akureyri into Eyjafjörður, the longest fjord in Iceland and one of the most beautiful, surrounded by mountains and dotted with small fishing villages. Eyjafjörður has the highest whale spotting percentage of all fjords in Iceland so this is where to go if you really want to see whales. Humpback whales are the most common and you might also spot dolphins and minke whales."}]}]}

title: Wondrous canyon river
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Studlagil was discovered only a few years ago. It was created since the waterlevel dropped when a dam was built. You can view it from both sides of the river. To be able to walk inside of it you have to park on the east side of the river and walk for about 40 minutes.\nBefore you drive here, make sure to put this pin into google maps:\n"},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://maps.app.goo.gl/XgdFJXtn4tkQJ2iV8","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":"text-title underline"}}],"text":"https://maps.app.goo.gl/XgdFJXtn4tkQJ2iV8"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There's no phone signal in the valley so you need to have the correct pin before you enter. The pin will lead you to the first parking lot (at the bridge). You can continue driving over the bridge for another 10 minutes, then you find the main parking lot and from here it's 40 min walk to the canyon."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you're short on time or you don't want to walk, park at \"Stuðlagil Canyon Main Access\" on Google Maps which will take to you the west side of the canyon with views from a platform."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note: in late summer/early autumn water from the dam upstream is released, turning the water grayish-brown. It is usually back to it's stunning turquoise color in October."}]}]}

title: Cozy Cabin, perfect for northern lights
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Heathland Lodge is an epic mirror cabin in the golden cirlce area. They are located in an open field which makes them perfect to watch northern lights. There's very little light pollution around which is important for the northern lights. It's also so cool when the northern lights are reflecting in the mirrors at night."},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","text":"The cabin itself is also incredible. There's a private hot tub, sauna and small kitchen. I highly recommend to stay here if you are visiting Iceland!"}]}]}

title: The canyon of waterfalls
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The canyon of waterfalls. To get here you need to park along the road that leads into Landmannalaugar and go for a short walk. It's about 20 min flat hike to get to the viewpoint of the valley."}]}]}

title: Scenic hidden waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This two level red waterfall is hidden deep into a valley. The hike up there is about 1.5 hrs one way and leads you through large lupine fields if you do the hike in summer."}]}]}

title: Photogenic icebergs
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Another very famous spot of Iceland and a must see. Jökulsárlon is the main glacier lagoon of Iceland and by far the biggest one. The icebergs usually gather just at the viewpoint since the get stuck there before they melt and continue out to the ocean. Because of the current, they move around and you can hear them crashing into each other and flipping."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"On a nice day you could spend a very long time here. There are restrooms and food trucks on site and across the street is Diamond Beach."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A number of tours meet here as well, mainly for the nearby ice caves and boat rides on the lagoon."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is also a favorite spot for capturing the Northern Lights when the forecast looks good."}]}]}

title: Iceland's oldest pool
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The oldest pool of Iceland, used to teach Icelanders how to swim back in the days. Today it's open for anyone who want to enjoy the hot waters. There's a simple hut to change in and the hop in. The water is about 37 degrees so not the hottest pool but certainly one of the best settings in a lush green valley. From the parking lot it's about 20 min hike to get to the pool."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Since the water is not that hot, it's best to visit in the summer."}]}]}

title: Visit Iceland's best ice cave
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The most blue ice caves of Iceland are found in Vatnajökull glacier. The crystal ice cave has incredible clear and blue ice and if you want the best ice cave experience this is the one you want to visit. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you are visiting in winter, try to go at sunset. On a clear day the sun shines at the opening of the cave making the ice look like gold"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The ice caves in Vatnajökull are only accessible in winter and by super jeep. I recommend going with Arctic Adventures, they always have great guides that creates a good adventure."}]}]}

title: The red waterfall of Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is the red waterfall of Iceland and the 3rd highest overall. There's a built up trail going to the base of the waterfall. It gives you a nice view and the hike is easy but uphill almost entirely. For even better views, you can hike up on the left side and get to the very top of this waterfall. It will take you about 1 hr one way to reach the top and the hike is quite steep at times. On the way up you pass another really cool waterfall as well, Litlanesfoss."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Be careful around the edges as the rocks are loose and can break off."}]}]}

title: Cozy northern lights tours
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"To be able to see the northern lights it's important to get away from the light pollution of the city. A great way to do this is to hop on a boat from Reykjavik harbour and sail out to sea. That way you get unrestricted views 360 degrees around you. if the lights come out, you wont miss them."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The great benefit by going on a a boat with Special Tours is that they have a large seating area inside and even a cafe. So you can stay warm and cozy and when the crew says the lights are visible, you simply go outside to watch them. "}]}]}

title: A beautiful black beach
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a parking lot stop along the ring road at a black beach. It's always windy here so layer up and go for a walk on the beautiful beach."}]}]}

title: Drone Spot for River Braids
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Kirkjubæjarklaustur has some really nice river braids just next to the city. They are easy to capture with a drone. Just keep in ind that the locals aren't to excited about you flying drones from their land so be respectful of where you park and fly from. "},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","text":"A good idea is to book a stay at Magma hotel, then you can fly from your cabin :)"}]}]}

title: The best campervans of Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Camp Easy has the best vans of Iceland, no doubt about it. I specially recommend the 4x4 vans so that you can go into the highlands. This gives you access to so much extra spots to explore."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note that the CampEasy pick-up and drop-off office is 5.5 km from the airport. They offer free shuttle service to/from the airport and nearby hotels only during their office hours (8 am - 4 pm). If you are picking up or dropping off outside of the office hours, you will need to arrange alternative transportation to/from the airport."}]}]}

title: Hidden hot spring waterfall shower
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Have you ever dreamt of showering in a hot spring waterfall? Then this is where to go. You'll need a 4x4 to get there as the road is very bumpy. It's also a bit tricky to find the way, about 10 min away from the waterfall you need to turn left away from the \"main\" road. The crossing is hard to find but if you keep an eye out for it you will find it."}]}]}

title: Walk behind this towering waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Together with Skogafoss perhaps the most famous waterfall of Iceland. This is a beast with lots of water and a path that goes all around the back of it. Bring a waterproof jacket, you will get wet."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"In the winter the path will become very icy and possibly inaccessible. Be sure to wear crampons so the you can walk around the area."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Seljalandsfoss is a must-see destination for tour groups so expect crowds."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are two parking lots - paid parking"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Toilets and a small cafe/gift shop on site."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"After visiting Seljalandsfoss, don't forget to walk up to Gljufrabui."}]}]}

title: Lupine Field with mountain backdrop - late June to early Aug
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Lupine Field with mountain backdrop, next to the main road and easy to park at"}]}]}

title: The Game of Thrones Arrowhead mountain
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The most iconic mountain of Iceland. Best shot with the waterfall in front or northern lights in the background. If you are driving Snaefellsnes peninsula this is a must stop."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may notice on "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"http://safetravel.is","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":"text-primary underline"}}],"text":"safetravel.is"},{"type":"text","text":" that Kirkjufell is closed because of winter conditions... don't worry, this just means that hiking on Kirkjufell itself is closed. You can still go to the Kirkjufellsfoss parking lot to get great photos."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"After passing the falls, continue along the path until you can see Kirkjufell's reflection in the water."}]}]}

title: The Blue Lagoon of the North
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is the blue lagoon of the North. It's a bit smaller than the one outside Reykjavik but the experience is very much the same. Well worth a visit!"}]}]}

title: Astounding canyon views
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This canyon used to be one of my best secrets but it has gotten more known over the last years. It's still a bit tricky to find though. You need to find the small dirt road that turns off the main road and follow it to the end. The road can be very bumpy and rocky and a 4wd is best but not required. There's a small parking lot and a path that leads up the hill. Follow this one for about 45 min and you get to the main view point. The trail leads further but no need to walk higher up as this is the best view you will get over the canyon."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Best experienced from April - October."}]}]}

title: Reykjanesviti Lupine Fields
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reykjanesviti Lupine Fields"}]}]}

title: A beautiful mountainous beach
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is another of my favorite spots in Iceland. The large beach and the iconic mountains are so beautiful. You need to pay small entry fee at the cafe to access the area."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There a few points of interest here. The dunes and super flat beach and are only a few minutes walk from the parking area/cafe. This is also the spot for great photos of Vestrahorn."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you'd like to go all the way to the rocky cliffs to see the lighthouse and waves crashing near the huge radar station, pay the fee at the cafe and drive down the road for 5 minutes before turning left towards the parking area (just before the gate). The walk to the lighthouse is another 10 minutes from the car park."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you have more time here, grab a small map from the cafe and follow their walking/hiking trails."}]}]}

title: Tour the Highlands year-round
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is one of my absolute favourite day tours of Iceland. Þórsmörk is such a beautiful valley and it's always an adventure to get there. The best thing about it is that you get away from most of the crowds. This is because you need a super jeep to cross the rivers to get there."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It's an adventure in it's own only to ride in a super jeep while crossing deep rivers. You'll also go hiking, explore waterfalls and canyons."}]}]}

title: The 5 Million Star Hotel
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Buubble hotel is a fun experience but not the most comfortable. If you get northern lights while staying here you're up for an amazing experience. There's however no sun shade so they can get very hot in the morning if the sun is up. There's also no toilets by the bubbles so you'll need to walk a few hundred meters to get to them."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may book a Buubble, without an accompanying tour, only from mid-April thru August. All other stays at Buubble require a guided tour to be booked in conjunction."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are two Buubbles:"}]},{"type":"bulletList","content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hroshagi in Reykholt, closer to the Golden Circle sights (pinned here)"}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Ölvisholt near Selfoss and closer to Road 1"}]}]}]}]}

title: The place to stay in the East fjords
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you're traveling to the East fjords this is where I recommend to stay. It's a beautiful place and they have a great hot spring. It's also a very good northern lights location in winter."}]}]}

title: A graceful waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This waterfall is a nice little detour when driving in or out of the highlands."}]}]}

title: Mother Nature's Sculpture
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"As you can see this rock looks just like an elephant. The best way to see it is to go on a boat tour with Ribsafari."}]}]}

title: Thorsmörk Viewpoint Hike
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is the main hike from Volcano Huts and offers a stunning view over Thors Valley.\nIt's about 1 hour hike one way to get to the view point and 45 min hike back."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note that to get to the start of this hike, you need a super jeep to make the Krossá river crossing. It's not recommended to do this without experience."}]}]}

title: Cloud layer that covers the fjord
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"On summer days it's common with inversion in the evenings, meaning that a cloud layer comes in from the ocean and covers the fjord. If you drive up the mountain pass you get above the clouds as the sun is setting."}]}]}

title: The perfect morning swim
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a hidden gem and perfect for a morning swim. The pool is crystal clear but also very cold. The road in here is a little bumpy and then you need to walk 5 min from the car park to reach the fall."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The first fall you'll reach is manmade with concrete barriers... keep walking upstream to reach Skutafoss."}]}]}

title: Exciting dog sledding
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Go dog sledding with real huskies, it's a really nice experience. Afterwards you get to play with the dogs and in winter they usually have husky puppies too!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you're visiting from June - October, the dogs will be pulling you on dry land as you're seated on a cart with bicycle wheels, rather than over snow with a sled. Tours over snow are available from November - April."}]}]}

title: A frozen lagoon
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"In winter Heinabergslon often freezes over. This makes it possible to walk out on the ice to the icebergs. Be careful walking here alone and without safety equipment. The ice is usually thick close to the icebergs and if you go through you have to have ice spikes and rope to come back up. Go with a guide that knows the lagoon and can guarantee your safety."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I recommend reaching out to Stepman, he's the best guide in the East: https://www.stepman.is/"}]}]}

title: Best Car Rental
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"My favourite car rental company is without a doubt Hertz Car Rental!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For 10% discount applied at checkout, click the link below: "},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://www.hertz.is/asasteinars-hertz/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":"text-title underline"}}],"text":"https://www.hertz.is/asasteinars-hertz/"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hertz is one of few rental companies that have a counter inside the airport, so you'll be much faster at picking up your vehicle on arrival."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"They also have an extensive coverage around Iceland and a very good customer service. Most (If not all) of the cars come with unlimited mileage and zero hidden cost."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The unlimited mileage is a huge plus in Iceland as it can always be so tempting to drive down just one more road!"}]}]}

title: The Red Beach
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Raudasandur means red beach in Icelandic and that's exactly what this is. A large red beach. It's great for drone photography. Next to the beach is also a very cozy campsite for those travelling by camper van."}]}]}

title: Breathtaking drone shots
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Famous spot to drone and get a cool shot of the fjord. Keep in mind that it's usually windy here so check the forecast before flying out. Be extra careful if the winds are off shore, the drone might not be able to come back."}]}]}

title: The most thrilling way to see Vestmannaeyjar
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The best way to explore Vestmannayejar is by rib boat. Ribsafari takes you around to see all the caves and the elephant rock."}]}]}

title: One of the Wonders of the World
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Blue Lagoon hardly needs an introduction. Even though it's quite a touristy place with large crowds it's still a super nice experience. The blue, silica rich water is perfectly tempered and very soft on the skin. Make sure to book your entry in advance as the blue lagoon often is sold out in peak season."}]}]}

title: A quick little glacier stop
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The closest glacier to Reykjavik and a good option if you're short on time but still want to visit a glacier. Park at the parking lot and follow the trail on the right hand side of the lagoon. It takes 20-30 min to walk all the way to the glacier. It's a flat and easy walk and you don't have to go the whole way, you can also see it from the distance for a better overview."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Many excursions and tours meet at this parking lot - it's a popular spot for glacier hiking."}]}]}

title: Black Sand Beach
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is one of the most famous beaches of Iceland. Some think it's the only black beach, that's not true. However it is one of the most spectacular."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Be extra careful on days with big waves and pay attention to the warning signs. This beach has sneaker waves that can be 5x as big as regular waves and come up without warning. Tourists have died here because they got swept away by a sneaker wave and drowned. Stay away from the waves but do enjoy this amazing beach."}]}]}

title: Iceland's best infinity pool
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hands down the best infinity pool of Iceland. With a view over the fjords you can sometimes see whales from up here."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may purchase beverages, beer & wine while soaking from their swim up bar."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There is a private changing room but the showers, which you must use before getting in the water, are open."}]}]}

title: The Waterfall of the Gods
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The waterfall of the gods. This is one very impressive waterfall with lots of water, especially in winter when it's covered in ice and super impressive. There's a small path leading down to the river for more angles of it."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are parking lots on both sides of the waterfall, both with great views. They are connected via a bridge."}]}]}

title: Great waterfall for pictures
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Great waterfall just 5 min hike from the parking. Very easy to get to and good for pictures."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Definitely stop here if you're staying at Fosshotel Glacier Lagoon."}]}]}

title: Great drone destination
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Drone photo location in the highlands. You can't get here by car, only fly in with the drone."}]}]}

title: Super Cozy Cabin
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Great place to stay at the south coast. There are 8 cabins and each one is super cozy and hosts up to 4 people. Each cabin is equipped with a small kitchen (microwave, dishwasher, & refrigerator)."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Prices range from 300 - 700 Euros, depending on season."}]}]}

title: Sea kayaking
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Glide through the calm waters of the fjords. Sea kayaking is best in Isafjördur."}]}]}

title: Famous river crossing in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Krossá is the most famous river crossing of Iceland. Every year it will sweep a few cars away and sometimes even large buses, so drive carefully and always talk to the locals at Volcano Huts before crossing. They will tell you if it's safe or not. Also make sure you know how to cross rivers, this river is deep and strong!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may also book a ticket on the river bus with stops at Hvolsvöllur, Brú Base Camp (near Seljalandsfoss), and Þórsmörk. Booking a ticket on the river bus will allow you to visit and hike the Þórsmörk area as a day or overnight trip, staying at Volcano Huts."}]}]}

title: Famous sword road
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Famous road in Iceland, best captured from above with drone"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"DO NOT STOP ON THE BRIDGE - Pull off of the road on the western side of the bridge and walk."}]}]}

title: Small waterfall and pool
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"When camping at Þakgil, take a walk in the morning to the small waterfall and pool. Follow the stream for 10 minutes and you'll find it. The water is ice cold but fun for a morning dip."}]}]}

title: Cozy cabins with hot spring
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Best place to stay on Snaefellsnes. They have very nice cabins and an amazing hot spring and are a great northern lights spot."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"They have a variety of self-catered options, cottages, houses, and apartments, most of which include a washer/dryer."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There is also a restaurant on site."}]}]}

title: The Yellow Lighthouse
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The only yellow lighthouse in Iceland."}]}]}

title: Spot the Arctic Fox
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The arctic foxes can be hard to spot, so if you want to be sure to seeone you can visit this rescue centre"}]}]}

title: Stunning Hot Spring
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"From the blue eye this hot spring to swim in is created. Great stop when you are coming in the highlands and need a shower."}]}]}

title: The Pinnacle of Luxury
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is the ultra high end version of the regular Blue Lagoon. It's an amazing 5-star hotel and you can also buy day access to the pool and spa. The pool of The Retreat is carved through the lava field with many secluded areas. You are likely to find areas where you are all alone, very different to the regular blue lagoon. Don't miss out on the spa and 4-step ritual, it's a must try.\nEntry fee depends on season but is usually from €300 for 2 guests."}]}]}
locationName: The Retreat at Blue Lagoon Iceland

title: Bathe with scenic views
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a very scenic pool and hot spring. It has indoor changing rooms and the water temperature is perfect. There's one big pool and a smaller hot spring style pool closer to the ocean. At high tide you can also go for a cold plunge in the ocean."}]}]}

title: Cute hot spring at the edge of Westfjords
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The road driving to or from the Westfjords can be boring but this is your highlhgt along the way!"}]}]}

title: Steamy and bubbling geothermal area
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A geothermal area with bubbling mud pools and lots of steam coming out of the ground."}]}]}

title: School bus cafe
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If the highly regarded Michelin Guide featured cafes this one would get 3 stars! Skool Beans Cafe is my favourite cafe along the Ring Road\n.\nIn a converted yellow school bus they serve up perhaps the best coffee of Iceland ☕️, so start your day here before hitting the road."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Closed from December through February."}]}]}

title: Waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A tall waterfall and a nice little hike to go on"}]}]}

title: A stunning and accessible waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Kvernufoss is a short 15 min walk away from the parking lot. There has recently been built a path so it's very accessible and you can walk all the way behind it and look out."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It is far less crowded than Skogafoss but it is gaining in popularity. In winter, the trail and path behind can become dangerous - bring crampons!"}]}]}

title: Great place for a nice dip
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Landmannalaugar is famous for its hikes so this hot spring is really pefectly situated. There's nothing better than finishing the day here"}]}]}

title: Best view of the fjords
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The myth says you can see over to Greenland here on a clear day. If that's true or not you will have to see for yourself, it is some great views either way"}]}]}

title: Cozy hot spring
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perfectly tempered and cosy hot spring close to Hotel Heydalur. It's a small one so make sure to go max 2ppl."}]}]}

title: Refreshing River Dip
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you camped in Skaftafell, then start the day with a cold plunge in the river. It will wake you up for sure. This is just a 10 min walk from the campsite. Pass the first small river you come to and continue 100m to the second one, there's a good spot to get in to the water."}]}]}

title: One of the best campsites in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"One of the best campsites in Iceland. From here there's lots of hikes and on a clear day you'll see the highest peak of Iceland, Hvannadalsnukhur."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"They are open year round and provide a variety of camping options (tents, cars, campervans and with or without electricity)."}]}]}

title: Tip of Westfjords
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Drone spot to capture the tip of the fjords"}]}]}

title: The North's cutest town
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The cutest town of the north. At the tip of Troll Peninsula and surrounded by steep mountains. This is one of the best places to go ski touring in winter."}]}]}

title: Spectacular drone location
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Drone photo location in the highlands. You can't get here by car, only fly in with the drone"}]}]}

title: Epic hidden waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Drive past Seljalandsfoss a little bit and you'll find this epic waterfall. It's off the normal path for most tourists so it won't have the crowds. It's a short hike, about 10 min from where you park to get here."}]}]}

title: Delightful hot spring
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"When driving to the Westfjords this is the first hot spring you come to. I always make a stop here after a long drive and relax for a bit."}]}]}

title: Crossable waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Rent a bike from Originalnorth glamping and cycle over to this waterfall"}]}]}

title: Unique Hotel Experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The main hotel of the blue lagoon. With this one you get entry to the main lagoon but also a private one only for hotel guests. The breakfast is also truly amazing"}]}]}

title: Black sand and grassy dunes
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a large beach and great drone spot. It looks spectacular from above. The road here is a bit bumpy so drive carefully"}]}]}

title: A tall and thunderous waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"One of the most impressive waterfalls of Iceland with a total height of 100 meters. If spreads out and create a wall of water flowing down. Below it is another 5 waterfalls that is also really impressive. You park at the bottom of go for a short hike up to Dynjandi, it will take you about 20 minutes. Remember that Dynjandi is a no drone zone, you need permit to fly here."}]}]}

title: Glamping tents surrounded by nature
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Glamping tents out in nature. They are only open in summer. They also have electric mountain bikes so you can go for a tour around to Ullarfoss waterfall and cycle through the forest."}]}]}

title: Recharge in Siglo
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you're in Siglo, this is where to stay. Great comfort and also a very nice hot tub outside"}]}]}

title: Photogenic Church
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The church overlooking the town of Vik. Head up the hill behind the chuch for the best views!"}]}]}

title: Green canyon river
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A canyon river with very green water. It's specially impressive in winter when the rocks surrounding it is snow covered."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a hidden gem and you won't find many crowds here and thus there is only a small parking lot and no official path. Explore the area near the bridge and be careful when climbing down to get closer to the water."}]}]}

title: A glowy and sparkly beach
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"When icebergs from the glacier lagoon float out to sea they eventually wash up on the beach. Here they lay until they melt or the next tide takes them away again. The contrast between the clear ice and black sand beach make them glitter in the sun. Best time of day is to visit at sunset, then they really glow and sparkle."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The largest \"diamonds\" are at low tide."}]}]}

title: Great drone location
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Drone photo location in the highlands. You can't get here by car, only fly in with the drone."}]}]}

title: My favorite glacier tongue
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is my favorite glacier tongue to visit since you get so close to the ice. Definitely do the hike (20 min each way) as it's absolutely worth it."}]}]}

title: Secret viking ship hot spring
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a secret gem. The viking ship hot spring is super cool and there's also another 3 tubs with different warm water. It's located right on the beach so if you get too warm you can easily go for a dip in the fjord."}]}]}

title: Dramatic photogenic pier
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a really cool pier on a white sand beach. Great for a casual walk and also good for images with the dramatic fjord mountains in the background."}]}]}

title: Traditional Icelandic turf house
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"An old traditional turf house, great for photos."}]}]}

title: Viking Village Prop For Movie
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a left viking village from a film set. Still very authentically made and a fun place to visit."}]}]}

title: A towering waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This used to be the highest waterfall of Iceland. Now it's the second highest after a new one was created after a volcanic eruption. With 198m in a narrow canyon it's still very impressive. You have to hike to it and up around it for the best views. It takes about 3 hours round trip from the parking lot and is a perfect day activity if you're staying in Reykjavik or just passing by on the Ring Road."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The trail has two river crossings. One where you walk across a log at the bottom, and the second one you cross the river above the waterfall by taking your shoes off."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The log is usually taken away sometime in October. You can check the status of the log on safetravel.is."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The trail is steep on the right hand side of the waterfall and can be slippery if it has been raining recently. Good hiking shoes is recommended!"}]}]}

title: Secluded hot springs retreat in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hvammsvik Nature Resort & Hot Springs is a hidden gem near Reykjavík, Iceland, offering eight natural hot springs that flow into the Atlantic Ocean. Nestled amidst stunning mountains and black sand beaches, this unique retreat promises a revitalizing experience away from the crowds. Guests can stay in one of the four charmingly renovated houses, indulge in Nordic cuisine at Stormur Bistro, and enjoy various activities like ocean swimming and kayaking. With its secluded location and authentic charm, Hvammsvik is the perfect place to unwind and connect with nature."}]}]}

title: Hallgrímskirkja
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The main landmark in Reykjavik"}]}]}

title: Picturesque waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a big waterfall and just along the ring road. In spring when the waterflow is high it's definitely worth stopping. You hike up to it in less than 10 minutes from the parking lot."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Beware - there is another waterfall in Iceland with the same name but it is in the Westfjords, very very far away from this one. I advise you to click 'Directions to Rjúkandafoss' below instead of typing it into Google Maps yourself."}]}]}

title: The Highland's biggest waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The biggest waterfall of the highlands. You will need a 4x4 to get here as the road is very bumpy. You park at the top and then there's a steep trail leading down to the bottom of it."}]}]}

title: Reykjavik downtown
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Full of bars, restaurants and ice cream shops. Go for a stroll around on the shopping street, Laugarvegur."}]}]}

title: Road side hot spring shower
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The perfect feature for anyone driving a van. What's possible better than a road side hot spring shower?"}]}]}

title: Sudureyri - The birthland of 66 North
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"One of the most remote fjords of Iceland. This is where 66 North was founded. Continue past the town to get to this old fishing hut. The drive there is also very scenic. In winter you may need a 4x4 car."}]}]}

title: Beautiful pier
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Beautiful photo spot in winter."}]}]}

title: The Game of Thrones lava cave
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Famous from Game of Thrones, this crack in a lava field is filled with steaming hot blue water. Unfortunately it's too hot to swim in but well worth a visit."}]}]}

title: Enchanting double waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A double waterfall with a calm lagoon in front of it. I have often gone for a dip here as it's safe and no current in the river."}]}]}

title: Lake Craters
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Myvatn lake is full of pseudo craters from old volcanic activity."}]}]}

title: Overlooking the black beach and cliffs
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is the small mountain overlooking the black beach and the cliffs. From the Vik side you can follow the road up and then go all the way to the edge of the cliffs. It's a windy hike but the views at the end are spectacular."}]}]}

title: A comfy scenic place to stay
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A fun accommodation just outside of Reykjavik with a private hot tub."}]}]}

title: A steaming red crater
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Gunnuhver is a steaming red crater on the Reykjanes peninsula. It has a constant flow of hot steam coming out of it."}]}]}

title: Beautiful lake viewpoint
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a very spectacular view. There's a parking lot with viewpoint when you drive the road into Landmannalaugar. For best pictures I recommend to send the drone up and go high."}]}]}

title: Stunning lava area
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A lava area with lots of basalt columns. It's a bit tricky to navigate so make sure to take a look at the map before you start walking. Kirkjan (the lava church), the cave in the photos above, is a highlight in the area."}]}]}

title: Red volcanic crater
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Next to Hekla is this Red volcanic crater. The drive there goes through a lava field and then you get to a parking lot. From here you can hike up to the crater or simply just drone from the car. It's about 1h hike round trip in lava so wear good shoes as it can be very sharp.\nOn the way in you will also see warning signs that Hekla is an active volcano and can have an eruption. If she starts you need to get out of the area quick."}]}]}

title: Photogenic Lighthouse
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Standing on the tip of Troll Peninsula, this lighthouse often sees big storms rolling in."}]}]}

title: Stunning lava formations
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Arnarstapi is a small town on the tip of Snafellsnes peninsula. It is shaped by old lava which has created very interesting formations by the water. The main photo location is the arch where you can stand on top looking out into the ocean."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Park at the Bárður statue and walk towards the sea."}]}]}

title: Safeguarding the steep rocky coastline
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Dramatic lighthouse."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Very bumpy road to get here - use caution if in a 2wd and reconsider visiting in the winter."}]}]}

title: Secret waterfall location
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"From what I know this waterfall hasn't got a name. So safe to say you won't meet many other tourists here. I go here a lot for product shoots when I need a unique location close to Reykjavik."}]}]}

title: Lighthouse by the rocky coastline
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Scenic lighthouse."}]}]}

title: Majestic rock formation
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It's a 30 min detour from the main road to get here. Once you parked there's a bit of a slippery path to get down to the beach and walk out to Hvitserkur. Bring good hiking shoes. On low tide you can walk around it. Do take a look to the opposite side of the river that comes out, sometimes there's a lot of seals!"}]}]}

title: Photogenic lighthouse
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Orange lighthouse with a great backdrop of photos."}]}]}

title: Cute and quirky Hotel
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Quirky and super cute hotel in Keflavik. They even have a small hot tub in their beautiful garden"}]}]}

title: Lava rock pool
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It looks tempting to get in for a swim but it's cold sea water and also quite dangerous as the waves break into the pool. It's however a cool spot and is best captured by drone."}]}]}

title: Charming waterfilled crater
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Kerid Crater is part of the golden circle and a very popular stop. There's a big parking lot with plenty of space and you need to pay a small fee to enter (pay per person). The walk to the crater is less than 5 min and once there you can either walk around it or all the way down to the water."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are steps up to the edge of the crater but once there the path is mostly gravel with a few rocky areas. To get close to the water you'll need to climb down a good number of steps."}]}]}

title: The Peak of Iceland - guided hike
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The highest peak of Iceland at 2106 meter above sea level. Up here there's big glaciers and always cold. Summer time you can hike to the peak with a guide, it takes about 14 hours up and down. You'll be walking on glacier so you need to be roped in with harnesses and crampons."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Available weekends in May & June, meet at Sandfell Öræfi"}]}]}

title: Great river braids viewpoint
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"An easy hike up the hill (15 min) will get you on top with good views all around and overlooking the river braids."}]}]}

title: Photogenic plane wreck
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This old US military DC-3 is on the beach of Solheimasandur. It looks close but the hike is longer than you think. Expect at least 1-1.5h each way to walk there."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may also choose to ride the shuttle bus (a 15 minute ride, one-way). The shuttle runs every 45 minutes.  Tickets for the shuttle bus may be purchased on site."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The trail and shuttle bus is available year round but summer is the best time to visit."}]}]}

title: Garðar BA 64
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"An old fishing ship on land"}]}]}

title: Colorful and breathtaking views
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a great spot for shooting lupin fields with snowy mountains in the background. They grow really tall on the side of the road up to the lake."}]}]}

title: A bird sanctuary island
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vigur island is a bird sanctuary. It has a fixed population of 3ppl that are Eiderdown farmers. They take well care of the birds on the island and will happily show you around if you get over."}]}]}

title: Hafnarfjördur Northern Lights spot
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you're in Reykjavik and want to see northern lights, this is an easy accessible spot with not so much light pollution. It also has a cool house that is great to put in the foreground to make your image more interesting."}]}]}

title: A pretty little lighthouse
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Lighthouse close to Bolungarvik."}]}]}

title: Hot spring overlooking the fjord
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is one of my absolute favorite hot springs of Iceland. It sits in a calm fjord and I'm usually alone here. Do go for a dip in the fjord as well!"}]}]}

title: A hidden black pebbled beach
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A hidden beach in between two old streams of lava. From a drone perspective you can clearly see how the lava has been flowing down on both sides of this black beach."}]}]}

title: A great little waterfall stop - road closed in winter
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This black waterfall is just along the road when driving from East fjords to Egilsstaðir. Make a quick stop and check it out!"}]}]}

title: Viewpoint over the towering lava pillars
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Go for a walk through the small forest to the viewpoint of Höfdi. From here you can see these strange lava pillars standing in the lake."}]}]}

title: Keeping watch over the black beaches
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The southernmost lighthouse of Iceland stands up on a large cliff. From here you have excellent views over wide stretching black beaches in both directions along the coast."}]}]}

title: Super nice hot spring & campsite
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Laugarfell hot spring is a super nice one next to the Laugarfell hut. They have changing rooms and showers and charge a small fee for using the pools."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You can also camp here for the night."}]}]}

title: Cool moody lighthouse
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A cool lighthouse at the tip of Reykjanes peninsula. Go here on a stormy day when the waves are splashing up. The pictures from here usually have a cool moody vibe to them."}]}]}

title: Geothermal playground
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is like a playground of steam and bubbling mud pools. Go around and explore and run through the large steam flows. If you drone this you'll find lots of interesting top down angles with cool patterns."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are strong sulfur smells here which you probably noticed when driving into the area from the east."}]}]}

title: Landmannalaugar Campsite
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Landmannalaguar is the main place of the highlands. This is also where you start/end the famous Laugarvegur trail. From Landmannalaugar there's plenty of shorter hikes to do and here's also a campsite and a hot spring. The most famous hike is to the \"rainbow\" mountains."},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"IMPORTANT"}]},{"type":"orderedList","attrs":{"start":1},"content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You have to reserve campsite in advance if you want to stay here or park for the day. It will get full and you wont be allowed in if you don't have a reservation. Reservation are made through parka on this site: "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://parka.is/parking/landmannalaugar/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":"text-title underline"}}],"text":"https://parka.is/parking/landmannalaugar/"},{"type":"hardBreak"}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There's a river crossing to drive all the way in to the campsite. You need a big 4x4 to pass this one and remember to always drive slow. Dusters or similar smaller 4x4s can make it but it's very common that this size of car drowns in the river. If you drown your car in a river, the insurance is not valid and you might be liable for the full cost of the car."}]}]}]}]}

title: Awesome nature museum with artificial ice cave
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perlan museum is a great place to visit while in Reykjavik, especially for kids and rainy days. You can easily spend a few hours here. All of their exhibits are awesome, especially the artificial ice cave you can visit all year around. - If you're unable to visit any other ice cave in Iceland, come here!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Plus, they also have a Northern Lights show in their planetarium included with your admission."}]}]}

title: Super cute arctic foxes
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Keep your eyes open and you may find a fox in the wild. If you want to be sure to see one, you can always stop by this Arctic fox rescue center and take a look at the ones they have, they are super cute!"}]}]}

title: Highland views just outside of Reykjavík
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Djúpavatn is a rather unknown area not far away from Reykjavik. It looks just like the highlands but is much more accessible. This is perfect for a quick trip from Reykjavik and go for some hikes. From Djupavatna you can hike over to the colorful valley of Trölladyngja."}]}]}

title: A cute little town
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Eskifjördur is a cute town in the East Fjords. The narrow fjords and steep mountain on the opposite side makes for good pictures."}]}]}

title: The Golden Waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The most famous waterfall of Iceland and part of the Golden Circle. This is a huge one with lots of water and spray. Because of tourism there's now big built up parking lots and restaurants around it.
