So it's not the best nature experience but in the end a very impressive waterfall."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are two parking lots and two view points..."}]},{"type":"orderedList","content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The upper/main parking lot is a further drive and takes you to the Panorama Restaurant Cafe & Shop. From here the upper viewing platform is an easy 5 minute walk and you'll get a great view of the surrounding landscape. The lower view requires a decent number of stairs and a 5 - 10 minute walk along a fairly narrow gravel path. Depending on the season, it may also be possible for you to get closer to the falls but this will require some light scrambling over rocky steps."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The lower/bus parking lot comes first along the drive and has quick and easy access to the lower viewing area. Climb the steps to the upper viewing platforms, restaurant, toilets, and gift shop."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Parking in both lots is free."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Crampons/microspikes are recommended in winter as the paths can become quite icy."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may also view Gullfoss from the east but the paths require extra caution and good hiking shoes."}]}]}

title: A great highland drone location
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Drone photo location in the highlands. You can't get here by car, only fly in with the drone."}]}]}

title: Tropical White Beach
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Most beaches in Iceland are black, so it's really fun to find a white tropical one. It looks really beautiful with the steep mountains as the backdrop and is perfect for a swim!"}]}]}

title: A photogenic Dock
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This old dock is a bit sketchy to stand on but a cool photo location. Take it easy when walking out there."}]}]}

title: Spectacular view over the black beach
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"In summer this is a good spot to see puffins. They nest in the cliffs and you can see them flying in and out to catch fish in the ocean. For the rest of the year Dyrholaey is a very good place to visit. You'll get a great view over the black beach and the famous arch in the cliff."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It can become very windy here and you may even get sprayed by the waves."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are two parking lots to view the area.  This particular spot is accessible from the upper parking lot as well as \"Dyrholaey Lighthouse\". The lower parking lot has toilets and is a short 5 minute walk to a different viewpoint where you may get splashed by the waves."}]}]}

title: A waterfall cave
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"When you're at Seljalandsfoss, don't miss out on this waterfall. It's a short walk away and super cool. Inside of a cave, the water falls from above. Bring a waterproof jacket, you will get wet in here."}]}]}

title: One of the prettiest churches of Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"One of the prettiest church of Iceland. Here you can sit in a flowerfield and watch as the airplanes from Keflavik take off right above you."}]}]}

title: An exceptional whale watching experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Husavik is the whale capital of Iceland and the best company to go with is North Sailing. They have an amazing old wooden sailboat that takes you out on a sail and whale adventure. This is highly recommended!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"They also have a silent whale watching tour which is environmentally friendly and minimizes the disturbance to the whales."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tour availability varies with the season but the only time you won't be able to tour with North Sailing is December - February."}]}]}

title: A secret waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This waterfall is a bit unknown so you are likely to be here alone. Great for pictures and drone pan outs from here are super cool!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The walk here and around is easy and it's accessible year round."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The road here is open to all vehicles as well."}]}]}

title: Gloomy black and red craters
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"On the other side of the road from Bláhylur you find these black and red craters. They can be hard to spot from the ground so a drone will really help."}]}]}

title: Old pool
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This pool used to be hot but is now quite cold. On summer days you can still go for a swim here."}]}]}

title: Floating infinity pools and more
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vök baths is a pearl in the East and a fantastic spa. In winter they usually carve up a hole in the ice so you can swim in the frozen lake and then go back into the hot pool."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Towels and swimsuits are available for rent."}]}]}

title: Lighthouse in a fjord
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Lighthouse in a fjord."}]}]}

title: Hotel with a hot tub roof
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hotel Berg has an amazing hot tub on the roof, with a really nice sunset view"}]}]}

title: A super cool crater lake
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Krafla is a geothermal area in the north and at the end of the road you'll find a waterfilled volcanic carter. This one is super cool and you can go for a walk around the whole crater. It takes about 30 min for a lap."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It's a bit difficult to find but just follow the road until it ends, driving under the power plant pipes."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note: the road may be closed in the winter"}]}]}

title: Whale watching
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The fjord outside Isafjördur probably has the most amount of whales in Iceland. Here you're almost guaranteed to see plenty of Humpbacks."}]}]}

title: Campsite in Húsavík - mid-May through September
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Campsite in Húsavik with a number of convenient amenities - grocery store, swimming pool, washing machine & free internet."}]}]}

title: A beautiful and scenic boat ride
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The ferry to Vestmannaeyjar pass a set of island on its way over. Look closely and you'll see that there's actually a few houses on them high up on the hills."}]}]}

title: A grand horse shoe shaped canyon
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Ásbyrgi is a horse shoe shaped canyon in the North of Iceland. It's vertical cliffs has created a protective environment and at the base of it a lush forest grows. Go for a walk all the way to the base and find a little lake. Or do the bigger hike and get all the way to the top for a grand view over the canyon."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you're coming here from Dettifoss, a 4x4/4wd is highly recommended. The roads from Dettifoss to Ásbyrgi  (Road 862 & 864) are only open in the summer. If you're coming from the North/Husavik, the road is open year round but could be very difficult to drive in the winter. As always, it is very important that you check road conditions and closures at "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://umferdin.is/en","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":"text-title underline"}}],"text":"road.is"},{"type":"text","text":"."}]}]}

title: The Black Waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The black waterfall. You reach it after 30 min hike from Skaftafell camping. It's a perfect morning hike if you wake up here after having camped. From here you can either go back or continue higher up, there's lots of nice hiking routes here. Before you start walking, check in with the visitor's center to see the map and get ideas on all the trails that are available here."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note that the hike to Svartifoss is almost entirely uphill and can be challenging for some."}]}]}

title: The biggest bird cliff of Northern Europe
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The biggest bird cliff of Northern Europe. This one is literally full of birds in summer and has lots of puffins. The drive is on gravel road so it will take some time to get here, keep that in mind when planning your trip."}]}]}

title: A photogenic A-frame cabin
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This photogenic A-frame cabin is an old sheep hut. It has a very dramatic backdrop which makes for good pictures."}]}]}

title: Watching over Reykjanes
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reykjanesviti is the old lighthouse of Reykjanes. Standing tall up on a hill."}]}]}

title: Dramatic sea stack cliffs
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The famous sea stack cliffs outside the black beach of Reynisfjara. You can watch them from the beach (Reynisfjara) or from the mountain just next to them (Reynisfjall)."}]}]}

title: A pleasing waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Faxafoss is part of the golden circle route and is easy accessible all year round. It's significantly less crowded than other waterfalls on the route but still very beautiful"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Pay for parking."}]}]}

title: A photogenic location
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A red house along the road to Egilsstaðir. This abandoned house is a cool photo spot, especially in winter when it's covered in snow."}]}]}

title: Ice blue river waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are two ways to get to Bruarfoss..."}]},{"type":"orderedList","content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Park at the FREE lot right along Road 37 (Laugarvatnsvegur) to the east of the bridge and walk roughly 1.5 hours. You'll pass two other waterfalls on the way. In winter and spring, the trail can become very icy and muddy."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Park at the PAID lot by turning onto the road east of the bridge. Pay via Parka app (no kiosks available) and walk along the flat gravel path for about 5 minutes. After visiting Bruarfoss you may walk the trail for about 20 minutes to see two other waterfalls before turning back to the parking lot."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Once there, you'll find an ice blue river carving through the rocks. A good tip is to shoot this with long shutter for a cool effect."}]}]}

title: Great drone photo location
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a classic drone shot from the East of Iceland. You need to fly far and high to get all the mountains into the frame. You can also angle the drone to the south and get the shot of the black beach. Be careful with winds so that your drone can make it back to land, it's very common with strong winds here."}]}]}

title: Beautiful lava field
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Walk through the lava field of Dimmuborgir. Autumn here is especially beautiful when all the low trees turn orange."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"In December, daily visits from the Icelandic Yule Lads are a highlight. It is said that this is where the 13 Yule Lads live"}]}]}

title: River Braids
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Cool drone spot just next to Seljalandsfoss."}]}]}

title: Grand highland views - open June through mid-September
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Þakgil feels like you are in the middle of the highlands, even though it's just 30 min from the Ring Road. The drive in is amazing with grand views. At the end of the road is a lush green valley and a campsite."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The road is accessible for all vehicles but it can be VERY bumpy. Use caution when driving here."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The campsite has a few bunk-style cottages available and a number of hiking routes nearby."}]}]}

title: Vast breathtaking views
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The sea cliffs on the south of Reykjanes peninsula drop straight into the blue ocean. This spot requires a short walk from the main road and has great views for pictures in both directions along the coast."}]}]}

title: Spectacular volcanic crater
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"These two volcanoes are a great stop when driving between Reykjavik and the north. On this stretch there's not much else to see so this is a great stop to go for a walk and stretch your legs. There's a trail all the way up to the rim of the crater that is really nice to walk up."}]}]}

title: Harbour Restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A classic harbour restaurant with really good food. Try the seafood tagliatelle, it's a favourite!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a great lunch spot with a convenient location before or after your whale watching adventure!"}]}]}

title: Large campsite in Höfn
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Campsite in Höfn. It's a big site so they rarely run out of space and they have a good number of amentities near by."}]}]}

title: Hot spring and sauna
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you stay at Volcano Huts campsite in Thorsmörk you get access to this hot spring and a sauna."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It is also accessible for non-guests for a small fee. Towel rental is available as well."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It's a great way to add some relaxation to your day of hiking around the area."}]}]}

title: Black sands and green mountains
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The area around Katla Ice cave is something else. Large black sand plains against a backdrop of steep green mountains. You can drive here with a good 4x4 in summer. However, do not try it in winter. Only Super Jeeps will be able to traverse the landscape in winter. The path in is a bit tricky to find, but consider following a tour guide driving guests to the caves."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note: the road can be VERY narrow at times and passing a Super Jeep tour can be precarious."}]}]}

title: A pretty orange lighthouse
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The lighthouse for the inlet to Akureyri."}]}]}

title: Lots to see here!
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a fun area on the south coast just outside Vik. The cave is often referred to as the Yoda cave. But there's more to do than just the cave. 50m to the right of the cave entrance there's a steep trail leading up the hill. If you're afraid of heights you should not try this one but if you don't mind vertical drops, it's a fun hike. You can also continue past the cave out on the black beach that seem to go on forever."}]}]}

title: Don't miss out on this beautiful drive
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Kleifarvatn is close to Reykjavik and an area many tourists miss. It's a really beautiful drive and there's actually a lot to see in this area."}]}]}

title: Famous Lobster Restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Pakkhús is famous for it's Icelandic lobster but really all food here is amazing. Vegetarian dishes are available too. They do NOT accept reservations and are often busy. Expect to wait for an available table, especially if you are coming for dinner and/or are a larger group."}]}]}

title: Amazing waffle cafe
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Amazing waffle cafe"}]}]}

title: A remote and super scenic location
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is one of my favorite remote towns of Iceland. It's pretty much as far away you can get from anything in Iceland and a very calm and quite place. However also super scenic and great for skiing and snowmobiling in winter."}]}]}

title: A grand cliff formation
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Next to the lighthouse is an impressive cliff formation shooting out of the ocean. Visit on a stormy day to see the waves splashing up."}]}]}

title: An icy glacier lagoon - smaller and less crowded
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This glacier lagoon is just next to the main one (Jökulsárlon). It's a bit smaller which means it's possible to take a boat tour and get all the way to the edge of the glacier. It's a sharp edge where the glacier tongue meets the water and feed icebergs into the lagoon."}]}]}

title: Breathtaking vast wilderness
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This area looks very similar to Landmannalaugar with the benefit that it's just outside Reykjavik and don't need a 4x4 to access. The road is a bit bumpy but if you drive slow any car can go there. From the parking lot, hike into the area and take a walk in this surreal landscape."}]}]}

title: A big black crater
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a big black crater with very fine sand. You can hike on the perimeter all around it, it's a really fun walk and takes about 1 hour. To get to the edge of the crater from the parking lot is about 15 minutes all uphill."}]}]}

title: Tectonic waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Öxarárfoss is a waterfall in Þingvellir National Park, Iceland. It flows from the river Öxará over the Almannagjá. The pool at the base of the waterfall is filled with rocks and is often extremely icy during winter.\nThe river flows in the crack between two tectonic plates and the path to take you to the waterfall is at the base of this very crack. The walk to get here from the Visitor's Center is roughly 15 - 20 minutes. There are some steps on the way to the waterfall but overall, the path is easily walkable."}]}]}

title: A tall majestic mountain
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"When driving the south coast you can't miss this majestic mountain (unless it's covered in clouds which it often is). If you want to go for a hike there is a small path leading to the top of it."}]}]}

title: Campfire Camping
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Campsite in Varmahlid. They have a fire area so bring wood if you want to make a bonfire at night"}]}]}

title: Walk in a deep lavafield crack
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Lambfellsgjá is a deep crack in the lavafield due to an earthquake. It's at the end of a very bumpy road, unless you have a big car expect about 50 min to drive here from the main road. From the parking lot it's about 20min walk through a lava field to get to the actual crack.\nIf you go here, make sure to check out Trölladyngja as well as it's close by."}]}]}

title: A grand and wide waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Gufu looks like a smaller version of Skogafoss. It's a wide fall and you find it on your way down to Seydisfjördur."}]}]}

title: Camping with a hot spring
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Campsite in Hauganes. Don't miss out on their amazing hot spring"}]}]}

title: Iceland's Most Remote  & Best Sushi Restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Iceland's best and definitely most remote sushi restaurant. If you pass Seydisfjördur do visit them but note they are only open in summer and certain days of the week."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reservations recommended."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Located in Hótel Aldan."}]}]}

title: Iceland's most picturesque town
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The most picturesque town of Iceland in one of the deepest fjords. The church is famous for its rainbow street."}]}]}

title: The Tomato Farm
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Fridheimar is a family run tomato farm that has became a very popular lunch stop at Golden Circle. They serve tomato dishes in all shapes and forms (and drinks)."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Be sure to reserve your table in advance, several months in advance if you're traveling in the summer. However, you may get lucky and find a free seat at the bar."}]}]}

title: Old volcanic craters
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Two old volcanic craters, easy accessible from the road."}]}]}

title: Scenic road
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Very scenic road along the cliffs. Great for droning."}]}]}

title: The church towering over Reykjavík
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The church overlooking all of Reykjavik. You can pay to get up into the tower to get a good view of Reykjavik."}]}]}

title: Iceland's Most Powerful Waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Dettifoss is the most powerful waterfall of Iceland. You can view it from both the east and west side. The west side is the most common one with a big parking lot and toilet facilities. The east side is a difficult and challenging road only suitable for a 4x4/4wd in the summer. It's about a 20 min walk from the west side parking lot to the waterfall and on this side you can also go see Selfoss waterfall. Do bring a waterproof jacket as the spray from the waterfall can make you very wet if the wind is from the wrong direction."}]}]}

title: Very cosy Farm Hotel
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"My favourite hotel in the Westfjords. They have 2 hot springs and a pool in a green house. It's a very cozy family vibe and they offer horse riding as well. Don't miss this one!"}]}]}

title: See the famous Icelandic puffins
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Ingolfshöfdi is a small island off the south coast. It can only be reached by tractor and the local farmer puts on tours every day in the summer season. The island is a sanctuary for birds and in summer this is one of the best puffin spots of Iceland."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Available from late May until early August, not on Sundays."}]}]}

title: Get to know the old fishing traditions
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This museum showcases the fishing traditions of the Westfjords. It's located in old traditional turf houses."}]}]}

title: Captivating mountainous fjord
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Olafsfjördur is a super beautiful fjord, specially in winter when it's snow covered. The steep mountains are amazing backdrop and it's also a really good are for ski touring."}]}]}

title: Swim in the oldest pool of Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"One of the oldest pools of Iceland. In high season they can be booked up so make a reservation in advance."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may rent a towel and/or swimsuit for a small fee."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Locker rooms are separated by gender but there are no private shower stalls."}]}]}

title: The Famous Yoda Cave
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Go inside and look out to see from where this cave got it's name."}]}]}

title: Weird but fun art farm
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a bit of a weird one but aslo quite fun. Samuel Jonsson was an artist and this is his work. Lots of animal sculptures and even a full size church"}]}]}

title: A great drone location
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a drone spot. The road is closed to get all the way out so send the drone from the main road."}]}]}

title: Meet the seals of Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A great seal spot along the the road. Go on low tide, they they are usually hanging out on the rocks."}]}]}

title: A picturesque waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I love this waterfall since it has a perfect bridge in front of it to shoot the car driving over. In summertime there's also lots of blueberries growing next to it that you can pick."}]}]}

title: Great Suites
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Very good location and great suites close to the black sand beach."}]}]}

title: Looking into Mother Nature's Eye
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A geothermal hot spring with very hot water. Too hot to swim in but a very cool shot with the drone from above."}]}]}

title: Remote Farm Accommodation
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is as remote you can get in Iceland. The valley has less than 10 houses and almost no phone signal. It's so quite here and the perfect place to fully relax. It's also very beautiful and a very cozy old farm to stay at."}]}]}

title: Iceland's most known geothermal area
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Geysir geothermal area needs no explanation. This is perhaps one of the most famous landmarks of Iceland. Although the official Geysir hasn't erupted since 2016, just a few feet away is Strokkur which erupts every 4 - 10 minutes. It sprays water up to 50m high, spraying those downwind with warm water. You can also go for a walk around the area as it has many bubbling pools and lots of steam coming from the ground. Don't miss the view point up on the hill, it's just a short walk away."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is another must-see stop on guided Golden Circle tours so be prepared for crowds."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Parking is free at Geysir Centre."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The paths in the area are mostly flat and gravel and the walk to the actively erupting geyser is just 5 minutes."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Geysir Centre is a great midday stop, full of gifts and food."}]}]}

title: A brand new volcanic formation
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The volcano that erupted in March 2021. For a while it was Iceland's hottest tourist attraction. It is now inactive again with no lava flow, but scientists say it's likely it will wake up."}]}]}

title: Impressive lodge in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Panorama Glass Lodge is hands down the most impressive accommodation in Iceland. Laying in the bed and watching northern lights in the winter is just magical. You also get your own private hot tub outside the cabin. Bring food as there's no restaurants near by but you have a good kitchen to cook in."}]}]}

title: Best fish buffet of Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you like fish, this is here to go. They have the best fish buffet of Iceland and also in the most genuine house. They have two seatings each evening and you must book in advance."}]}]}

title: A fantastic hot spring
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you're in a campervan, Grettislaug is a great stop for the night. There's a campsite just by it and then you also get access to this fantastic hot spring."}]}]}

title: An elegant wooden church
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a rare type of church in Iceland and very pretty. Located at the heart of Husavik."}]}]}

title: Holt Inn
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A great place to stay and a very nice jacuzzi with a view. Don't miss the walk down to the fjord and the pier"}]}]}

title: Pretty black waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"On the way up to Hengifoss you find this black waterfall. It's a nice surprise on the hike as it's a really nice one and the perfect pause halfway up the hike."}]}]}

title: A waterfall staircase
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You find this multiple step waterfall along the road down to Mjoifjördur. It's a bumpy gravel road to get there and then a 5 min hike to the waterfall."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"WARNING: The road is treacherous, steep, winding, sometimes very foggy and gravel. Do NOT attempt to drive here in the winter and a 4x4/4wd is essential."}]}]}

title: Lakeside campground - mid-May through September
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A super cosy campsite. It has lots of trees and you can park very close to the lake. Bring some firewood and you can make a bonfire on the beach in the evening. This campsite is very popular in summer. If it's full, go to the next one (Atlavik) just 1 km down the road."}]}]}

title: Old shipwreck
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Rent a kayak from Kontiki Kayak Tours and paddle out to this old shipwreck. Then continue in this mini archipelago around Stykkisholmur."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tour offered from mid-May through mid-September."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"NOTE: you'll meet your tour at the Kontiki Kayak Base right next door to Narfeyrarstofa."}]}]}

title: A great place to recharge
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The southcoast sadly doesn't have many hot springs so once you get to Hoffell this is a super nice stop. There's changing rooms, showers and 4 tubs."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Be sure to check their "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://glacierworld.is/#booknow","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":"text-title underline"}}],"text":"website"},{"type":"text","text":" for available booking times. Though you probably will be fine to arrive without a reservation (in the shoulder and off seasons), they may be closed for the winter."}]}]}

title: Hot spring in the middle of nowhere
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This hot spring is next to the road and a perfect stop when driving the Westfjords."}]}]}

title: Camper Van Rental
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Travelling by van is the best way to get around Iceland, that's how I always do it."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"My favourite company is GoCampers, they have great vans at good prices. I recommend to always start your trip by picking up a van from them."}]}]}

title: Skogafoss Campsite - open year round
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Campsite perfectly located in front of one of Iceland's most majestic and famous waterfalls. You won't find a better view to wake up to!"}]}]}

title: Vik Campsite - Open mid-May through October
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Great campsite on the south coast with a convenient location in town."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are also a few cottages on site but they must be booked in advance."}]}]}

title: Viewpoint over horse shoe river
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Viewpoint over the horse shoe bend river. Easy along the road stop for a cool photo. You will need a 4x4 to drive this road."}]}]}

title: Stunning cabin close to Reykjavik
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"One of the best cabins of Iceland. You're all alone out in nature with a fantastic view over the lake. The hot tub is perfect and the cabin very nice and stylish."}]}]}

title: High quality hotel with a nice restaurant, hot tubs & sauna
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Great hotel close to Glacier Lagoon"}]}]}

title: Iceland from above
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a great way to see Iceland from above and really experience much of the incredible nature in one go. It's also great inspiration before you head out on a road trip around the country to see what sights there are to visit. I highly recommend this one!"}]}]}

title: The main shopping street
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Take a walk down Laugarvegur, the main shopping street of Iceland that takes you through the old town"}]}]}

title: Spa on the Golden Circle
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Along the Golden Circle, this is a great stop to heat up. It's also a great alternative to Blue Lagoon, has a beautiful view of the lake (be sure to hop in the lake for a cold plunge), and provides a good look into local pool culture. This spa is also one of the most kid-friendly."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are two heated pools, a hot tub, steam room, and sauna."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Prices are reasonable and kids under 12 are FREE! Towels and swimsuits are available for a small rental fee."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may also book a short demonstration and tasting of rye bread that is baked in the ground - heated geothermally overnight."}]}]}

title: Food court in a greenhouse
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recently opened, this hotel has a really nice food court. Perfect for both lunch and dinner. It has 7 different restaurants, a bar, shopping, and a local market."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The food court is open to all."}]}]}
locationName: The Food Court at Greenhouse Hotel

title: The main pool in Reykjavik
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is the oldest pool in Reykjavik and well worth a visit. This is where the locals come after work to relax and meet up with friends."}]}]}

title: Beach in Reykjavik with hot tub
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The only beach in Reykjavik. And of course there's a geothermal hot spring as well so that you can take a dip in the cold fjord and warm up afterwards."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is also a very popular local hangout spot!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Pay a small fee and you'll have use of the changing rooms, shower, and hot spring. Swimsuits and towels also available for rent."}]}]}

title: Best sushi in town
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you like sushi, this is the best sushi in Reykjavik. And their cocktails are amazing too!"}]}]}

title: Turfhouse Restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A traditional Icelandic turf house overlooking Hallgrimskirkja. It also happens to be one of the best restuarants in Reykjavik serving local dishes."}]}]}

title: Fish restaurant in the harbour
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you want fresh seafood with a view over the harbour, this is where to go"}]}]}

title: The beluga whale of Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Two rescued Beluga whales live here to aclimatise themselves and get back to a free life"}]}]}
locationName: Sea Life Trust Beluga Whale Sanctuary / Sæheimar Aquarium

title: Waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This waterfall is not very known so there's a good chance you'll be alone here"}]}]}

title: Organic farm to table restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This an an amazing farm to table, plant based restaurant. Everything they serve they grow themselves. This is well worth a visit, perfect for a lunch stop."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Closed from November - April."}]}]}

title: Hotel Geysir
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perfectly located for the Golden Circle travelers, modern yet warm decor, airport & sightseeing transportation upon request, and a delicious restaurant on site. I always recommend travelers to stay here on their Golden Circle day."}]}]}

title: Þingvellir National Park
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Þingvellir is located on the rift between two tectonic plates, the Eurasian and the north Atlantic plate. They are drifiting apart by 2cm per year and you can see the effect of this at Þingvellir National Park. To see the top sights in Þingvellir National Park, walk in one of the cracks that are formed because of the rift (Almannagjá), visit Þingvallakirkja church and Öxarárfoss waterfall."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are a number of parking lots available but the lot most conveniently located is P1. Once you pay the parking fee at one lot, you may travel to the other parking lots without paying again."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Visitor's Center near P1 has a cafe, toilets and a gift shop and is a good place to get an idea of what Þingvellir National Park has to offer."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Game of Thrones has a few scenes filmed here and nearby P5 is the access point for Silfra snorkeling excursions."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Crowds at Þingvellir National Park are always high as it is an essential stop for guided Golden Circle tours coming from the city. Crowds are the highest around mid-morning."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The paths around Þingvellir National Park are accessible for most all times of the year with well-trodden and maintained paths. However, there are some stairs in areas which may prove challenging for strollers/buggies and wheelchairs."}]}]}

title: Local Burger joint and brewery
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Best burgers and beers in town"}]}]}

title: Vik Sundlaug
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The local geothermal pools in Vik. This is the perfect day to relax after a day on the roads."}]}]}

title: Best cinnamon buns of Reykjavik
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Best coffee and cinnamon buns of Reykjavik. Their pizzas are also delicious!"}]}]}

title: Forest Spa
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Newly opened spa just outside Akureyri"}]}]}

title: Oceanside Spa
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Newly opened spa just by the fjord."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are 8 hot springs, a steam room, and of course, the ocean for a quick cold plunge! Your admission includes the use of their paddle boards if its a nice day."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Men's and women's changing rooms, a private changing room, and not-entirely-private shower stalls. The least expensive admission option does not have any private facilities."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Sandals, thongs, flip flops, etc. are recommended as the ground from spring to spring is quite rocky."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may also visit Hvammsvik if you don't have a rental vehicle by booking a bus transfer to/from Reykjavik!"}]}]}

title: River Rafting
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"River rafting in one of the best foss of Iceland."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Available from mid-April to mid-October. Several tours available (rafting, kayaking, Midnight Sun tour, family friendly for ages 8 and up, etc.)"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Meet at Drumbó River Base. Towels are available for rent and you may pre-order lunch at their restaurant if you choose."}]}]}

title: Incredible ice cream and chocolate
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Omnom are famous for their chocolate but here in Reykjavik they also have a super good ice-cream shop. Each one is an art piece in it's own"}]}]}

title: Seal Lookout

description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This hot spring is tiny but really nice if you get it to yourself. The hot spring is warm and it's no problem to get in. Iceland has a law that is called freedom to roam which means that you are allowed to walk across any land (except for private gardens). title: Amazing secret beach
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a super cool hidden beach on Vestmannaeyjar. Start hiking from the Herjolfsdalur campsite on the main path between the stage. It will take you about 30 min to reach the ridge. From here the views are amazing in both directions.\nContinue along the ridge to the left and you will see a thin trail leading down the grassy hill. Follow it and you will come to a lonely house. The trail continues past the house and down the cliffs to the ocean. At the end you will reach this secluded beach where you are most likely to be all alone."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a very steep hike and can be extremely slippery when wet. The trail is not marked so be careful and only go if you are confident walking in steep terrain."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you are there on a good weather day, its absolutely worth it."}]}]}

title: Hidden gem in North Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Fosslaug Hot Spring is an idyllic and hidden gem located in North Iceland, offering travelers an unforgettable experience. The hot spring is surrounded by lush green landscape with a stunning view of the distant horizon. It features a cold-water stream flowing through the river and a mud floor, and the water is carefully designed to be conducive for soaking and relaxing. Visitors can enjoy a peaceful bath with a beautiful view without having to pay any entrance fees. The surrounding area near Fosslaug also offers other activities and attractions such as Reykjafoss waterfall, Kolugljufur gorge, Glaumbaer turf houses, Grafarkirkja turf church and more. It is a unique and serene experience suitable for anyone looking to get away from the hustle and bustle of life."}]}]}

title: Modern hotel with nature décor near Westfjords village
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hótel Horn is situated in the beautiful Westfjords village of Ísafjörður. This modern hotel offers guests free parking and brightly decorated rooms with flat-screen TVs and free WiFi access. The décor of the space is inspired by the surrounding nature, perfect for nature and scenery lovers. Additionaly, staff can help arrange transfers to and from Isafjordur Airport and tours of the area. Guests have the option of a public swimming pool, restaurants, and cafés within a 5 minute walk. The museum also only a 10 minute walk away. Guests can enjoy a Continental, Vegetarian, Gluten-free breakfast at Hotel Horn's sister hotel Torg, just 984 feet away. Make sure to stay at Hótel Horn for an enjoyable experience!"}]}]}

title: Unique and Relaxing Accommodation
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Blabjorg Guesthouse is a unique accommodation located in a former fish factory that has been completely renovated. Guests can choose from rooms with shared facilities to luxury apartments. It offers guests a stunning view of the fjord and good hiking and birdwatching opportunities. Those looking for an adventure can explore nearby attractions like Borgarfjordur Eystri, Unaos, Storurd and Stapavik Cove. At Blabjorg you're guaranteed to find peace and relaxation."}]}]}

title: Exploration and relaxation in Seyðisfjörður
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hotel Aldan is the perfect destination for travelers looking to experience the beauty of Seyðisfjörður. Each room is individually decorated and includes amenities like free Wi-Fi, a flat-screen television, and a mini bar. Onsite, guests can enjoy an à la carte restaurant serving traditional Icelandic dishes, as well as a café offering coffee and light meals. Parking is available on site. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The hotel has 4 different properties - the red building at the base of the fjord has two restaurants, a cafe and is the reception for all properties. The Old Schoolhouse (green building) as 2 apartments with washer & dryers. The Bank & Hotel Snaefell (post office) has regular hotel rooms."}]}]}

title: Explore Iceland's history and culture
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Bustarfell Museum is a great place for everyone to explore the fascinating history of Iceland! It has permanent and temporary exhibitions exploring art, culture and heritage. Visitors can learn about the history of the nation as well as other interesting cultural topics like ancient Icelandic deities and Viking history. The museum also houses a number of interactive activities which let kids and adults alike get hands-on with the exhibits in a fun and engrossing way. Whether you're an avid history buff or just curious about Icelandic culture, Bustarfell Museum offers something for everyone's interests."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Open in summer only."}]}]}

title: River braids drone spot
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is an amazing spot for shooting river braids with your drone. Go to the bridge and send your drone up from there.\nThe best time to shoot them is around low tide. At high tide the whole area can overflow with water and the braids don't come out. So make sure to time your visit with the tide."}]}]}

title: Hidden gem, few crowds, close to the city
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There's many black beaches in Iceland. This is one of the best ones close to Reykjavik and it's also very rarely crowded so you'll likely have it all to yourself!"}]}]}

title: Black Beach
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a an amazing black beach in the North. It's long and you're almost always alone here :)"}]}]}

title: Accommodation in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Puffin Hotel Vík offers a few different accommodation styles at a decent price - add breakfast to your stay for an easy meal, especially for early departures the next morning. Rooms are basic, nothing fancy, clean, and easy to find."}]}]}

title: Stay in Iceland comfort at Hotel South Coast
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hotel South Coast is a modern and well-equipped hotel conveniently located near a number of eateries. Breakfast can be included in your stay. Limited parking in the rear of the building and free street parking out front. A few EV charging stations are also available in the rear parking lot."}]}]}

title: Several dining options in one location
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Essentially a \"town square\", Selfoss Center is a great spot for grabbing a coffee, lunch, or dinner. There are multiple cafes and restaurants in this one area and on a sunny day, there's also great outdoor seating."}]}]}

title: Cozy Café - local favorite
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Pallett Café is a cozy, eclectic café just outside of Reykjavik. Definitely a local hangout and perfectly located for your journey from the airport to the city or Golden Circle."}]}]}

title: Pizza, burgers, fish, BBQ, chicken, nachos, beer & wine
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Conveniently located restaurant with a lot of options!"}]}]}

title: Peaceful getaway with stunning views
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Eyri Seaside Houses offer the perfect escape for those looking for a peaceful getaway and mesmerizing sea views. A great place to stay for Ring Roaders. This is a vacation rental, not a hotel. There are two apartments, each with two bedrooms. The first bedroom has two twin beds which are most commonly squished together to form a king-sized bed. The second bedroom is small with one twin bed. Beautiful views, well-equipped kitchen, and reasonable price!"}]}]}

title: Modern, well-priced, private, hotel with natural hot tub
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Basalt Hotel is a fairly new hotel with simple & modern, yet cozy, large rooms in the middle of nowhere. It fits perfectly into the landscape and is really a gem of a hotel. The location is very private and great for a night between visiting the Golden Circle & either Snæfellsnes Peninsula or the Silver Circle. The hotel has a hotel on site, serving both breakfast and dinner (nothing fancy but still yummy). The lobby is also a comfortable space to work, play games, and read. The cherry on top is the natural hot pot just a few steps away - a breathtaking spot to view the Northern Lights! Another great thing about this hotel is it's price point, especially when you consider all of the wonderful things this hotel has to offer! Beware that although the roads to get here are not F-roads, they are long and made of gravel. Drive carefully as you may not see any other cars along the way."}]}]}

title: Good hotel and restaurant nearby the glacier lagoon
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hali Country Hotel & Restaurant is an inexpensive and comfortable base for exploring the region of Vatnajökull in Iceland. The hotel is also home to the Þórbergur cultural center, which honors the work of the great Icelandic writer Þórbergur Þórðarson, so guests can explore some history and culture too."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Great location if you need to spend the night or eat near Jökulsárlón Glacier Lagoon."}]}]}

title: Luxurious 4-star hotel
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Umi Hotel is a luxurious 4 star accommodation option situated on Iceland's South Coast. With stunning ocean and mountain views, couples are sure to find this hotel the perfect base for their romantic getaway. Additionally, Umi Hotel provides an impressive terrace, restaurant, bar, and lounge."}]}]}

title: Ferry station to Vestmannaeyjabær
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Landeyjahöfn is a ferry port along the south coast of Iceland, perfect for visitors hoping to explore the popular Westman Islands."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The ferry ride is about 40 minutes each way and departure times vary throughout the seasons but the ferry does run year-round. You may board the ferry on foot and explore Vestmannaeyjabær on foot or, book a ticket to drive onto the ferry and have the comfort of your vehicle upon arrival to the island. The ferry does not stop at the smaller islands but you can get some cool photos of the islands on your way there and back."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You do not have to book tickets in advance but it can be helpful and a time saver. Beware that booking ferry tickets in early August can be challenging because of Iceland's annual multi-day music festival on the island."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"**You must arrive at the ferry terminal no later than 30 minutes PRIOR to your scheduled departure."}]}]}

title: Lovely little restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Næs is your best option for a great meal while in Vestmannaeyjar. The atmosphere is inviting and the food is delightful."}]}]}

title: Hop aboard the Amphibian boat to see the glaciers up close
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Experience the awe-inspiring beauty of Iceland's Jökulsárlón Glacier Lagoon with a boat tour. From May to mid-November, hop aboard an amphibian boat to get up close and personal with the incredible icebergs and vast glacier lagoon. The amphibian boat tour lasts 35 minutes."}]}]}
locationName: Amphibian Boat Tours - Jökulsárlón Glacier Lagoon

title: Local Icelandic cuisine and brews
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Kaffi Lára is a local favorite in Seyðisfjörður and perfectly situated right at the town's entrance. Stop by for a beer or a meal while in town!"}]}]}

title: Restaurant & Hotel
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hótel Húsafell is a great place to stay when touring the Silver Circle and West Iceland. It is also a great spot to grab lunch or dinner as they have three different dining options: a sit-down restaurant, a bistro lunch buffet, and a mini market for food on the go."}]}]}

title: Mystic lavafall oasis
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hraunfossar ('Lava Falls') is a breathtaking series of waterfalls located in West Iceland. As many waterfalls as there are in Iceland, this one is truly unique. The water pours out of the earth, finding it's way through the surrounding lava field. And, the water is a brilliant blue!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Continue along the walking paths to find another waterfall, Barnafoss, which is incredibly powerful."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Park in the desginated lot and stop in the small cafe for a treat or to use the restroom."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Mostly flat gravel paths lead to the viewing platforms. Climb the rocky steps for a view overlooking the entire area."}]}]}

title: Walk inside of a glacier via ice tunnels
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Explore the wonders of Langjökull Glacier with Into the Glacier and unlock a world of beauty and mystery. Home to the World’s Largest Man-Made Ice Tunnel, embark on an unforgettable journey deep within this stunning glacier."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The tour begins at Husafell Information Center (near Hotel Husafell). From there you'll ride in a specially modified glacier vehicle, put on crampons, and start exploring the tunnels and caves that have been carved into the glacier."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Suitable for all ages and available year round."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tour is approximately 4 hours long."}]}]}

title: Relaxing Icelandic Spa Experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Another great spa/hot spring in Iceland, Krauma is an idyllic geothermal bath and spa destination. It boasts five hot tubs, one cold tub, two steam baths each scented with its own oils, and a relaxation room adjacent to a warm crackling fireplace. Visitors can also enjoy a restaurant and bar on-site, as well as views of the beautiful Deildartunguhver hot spring and nearby Reykholt area."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Be sure to order a drink from the bar to enjoy while soaking. And, take a look at the boiling hot springs, Deildartunguhver, located on the edge of the parking lot."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note: there are no private shower stalls here - you must still wash thoroughly before entering the water. And, if you don't bring your own towel, they do have towels for rent."}]}]}

title: Beautiful pools with sauna & cold plunge
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Great alternative to higher priced spas - located at Husafell Activity Center, there are two geothermally heated pools, two hot tubs, a cold tub, and a sauna. Prices are less than other spas in the area but more expensive than most local swimming pools. Right next to these pools there is a jumping pillow for the kiddos (available for use in the warmer months)."}]}]}

title: Cozy cafe with delicious food
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This cafe is the perfect spot to re-fuel before or after your hike to Reykjadalur Hot Springs. Delicious coffee, cozy atmosphere, fireplace, cakes, beer and wine, and a small gift shop."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is also a great place to hangout for those unable to make the long, uphill hike to the spring."}]}]}

title: Geothermal bread baking experience at Laugarvatn Fontana
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"At Laugarvatn Fontana visitors can take part in a unique experience—baking bread using geothermal energy. Baked underground for 24 hours, the rye bread, known as hverabrauð, has a subtle sweetness and soft cakey texture. A pot of dough is carefully lowered into geothermally heated sand and left to cook underground until it turns a golden-brown. After it's unearthed, the fragrant bread is spread with locally made butter and topped with smoked trout. This culinary adventure offers visitors a taste of Icelandic history and a chance to try something truly unique!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You do not have to be a guest of the spa to do the tour."}]}]}

title: Farm-to-table restaurant & ice cream shop
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Efstidalur II is a delightful restaurant with easy access along the Golden Circle. The restaurant is upstairs and overlooks the cow's feed barn."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Downstairs you'll find delicious and fresh ice cream."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There is also a chicken coop and jumping pillow next to the parking lot for some added fun!"}]}]}

title: Great dining & shopping along the Golden Circle
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Geysir Centre is a great place to stop and admire the geyser while enjoying some tasty food at the same time! Several restaurants operate out of this building. As an added bonus, there's also a large souvenir shop with magnets, trinkets, lopapeysas and jackets."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Consider stopping here on your Golden Circle route for lunch or dinner. This is also a good, secondary option for dinner if you're staying at Hotel Geysir."}]}]}

title: Base Camp & accommodation for exploring the Highlands
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Volcano Huts Þórsmörk accommodation is the perfect place to explore the Icelandic Highlands! Located in the Þórsmörk Nature Reserve, this is a special place to nest and enjoy the most famous hiking trails in Iceland. Choose from rustic mountain huts, luxurious glamping tents, camping grounds, cabins and cottages. The Volcano Huts restaurant & bar provides hearty meals made from local produce! Volcano Huts is also the perfect base camp from which to explore the area with a number of hiking trails available for a variety of abilities and schedules."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"To get to Volcano Huts you will either need to hike in (via Fimmvörðuháls trail, starting at Skogafoss) or cross a river. See \"Krossá & Þórsmörk River Bus\" in the guide for more info."}]}]}

title: Djúpivogur Campsite
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Djúpivogur Camping Ground is a small campground but conveniently located on the Ring Road. There are also a few restaurant options very close by. To use the campsite you'll need to check in at reception at Hotel Framtid."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Some amenities like the shower and Wi-Fi cost extra and facilities may be locked if no one is there."}]}]}

title: Small, year-round campsite near airport
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vogar Campsite is a convenient place to stay while in Iceland. The campground is located between Keflavik airport and Reykjavik and has a few chalets/cabins for a more comfortable stay. Note that the chalets/cabins are for use with a sleeping bag."}]}]}

title: Exclusive hot pots in a canyon
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Soaking in the Husafell Canyon Baths is a unique experience. The baths require advanced booking and you will meet a driver at the Husafell Activity Center/Hotel Husafell. The hot pots are private, warm and set within a very beautiful canyon."}]}]}

title: Blue Lagoon's restaurant - relaxed fine dining
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Lava Restaurant is a one-of-a-kind dining experience famed for its incredible views of the Blue Lagoon. This restaurant features traditional Icelandic seafood dishes cooked with a modern twist. There is a regular menu, children's menu and a 4-course culinary adventure (vegan optional available, dinner only)."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reservations recommended."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Open for lunch and dinner daily."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Relaxed fine dining setting."}]}]}

title: Michelin-starred gourmet restaurant at Blue Lagoon
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Moss Restaurant, nestled within the Blue Lagoon Iceland resort in Grindavík, is an inviting blend of fine ingredients, culinary artistry, and elegant design. This Michelin-starred gourmet restaurant offers a range of set menus designed by its master chef Aggi Sverrisson featuring the freshest local ingredients turned into inventive timeless fare. Guests can also explore the unrivaled wine cellar with vintages from around the world or experience a special Guest Chef & Wine Pairing Series curated by expert chefs. Vegan menu available as well."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reservations required."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Smart casual attire."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Open for dinner Wednesday - Sunday."}]}]}

title: Convenient hotel on the Ring Road
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hotel Framtid located in Djupivogur is located conveniently on the harbor and on the Ring Road. Rooms are basic with some facing the water. Hotel Framtid also has a restaurant on site and optional breakfast. If you'd rather camp, check-in is here at reception for the campsite across the street."}]}]}

title: Luxury suites with great views and daily housekeeping
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Exquisite luxury apartments with impeccable style, fantastic location, fully equipped kitchen, and DAILY housekeeping. This place is the best accommodation available in Vik!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note: Signature Suites is just one of three types of accommodation offered by Hotel Vik i Myrdal, catering to a variety of budgets. They also have a hotel with almost 80 rooms and awesome apartments, similar to a vacation rental. All properties have great views and are within walking distance to the beach."}]}]}

title: Explore Reykjavik history and fun
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Árbær Open Air Museum was established in 1957, and offers a great way to learn about the history of Reykjavik and the architecture of its past. It consists of more than 20 buildings that have been relocated from central Reykjavik, where visitors can explore a town square, a village, and a farm. Summertime is especially fun as the museum is populated with domestic animals to interact with! Not to mention the range of exhibitions and events they host throughout the year. From craft days to vintage car displays and even Christmas exhibitions, this open air museum is a good place to stop during a nice day in Reykjavik."}]}]}

title: Delicious Icelandic Cuisine
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Restaurant Suður-Vík is a popular eatery in Vík. This establishment serves up delicious Icelandic cuisine with a focus on local flavors. The atmosphere is cozy and inviting, making it the perfect spot for a romantic night out or family dinner."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reservations are recommended during peak travel season. Send them an email or FB message to request a table."}]}]}

title: Iconic Icelandic hot dogs
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Bæjarins Beztu Pylsur is an iconic Icelandic restaurant that has been serving its delicious hot dogs for over 75 years. It is widely known for being the favorite spot for locals and travelers alike. You'll always find a long line here. Their hot dogs are made from Icelandic lamb and topped with a variety of toppings, including crispy onions, sweet mustard, and remoulade."}]}]}

title: Convenient restaurant in Kirkjubæjarklaustur
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Systrakaffi is a vibrant family-run restaurant in Kirkjubæjarklaustur. They have a diverse menu of local delicacies like Arctic Char with homemade cakes and pizzas."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Open year-round"}]}]}

title: Peaceful refuge in North Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Fosshotel Myvatn is the perfect place to get away from it all. Located in a stunning setting north of Lake Mývatn, this award-winning hotel offers 92 rooms filled with peaceful tranquility. Each room features stunning views of the surrounding nature and boasts almost entirely low-environmental impact materials. Fosshotel Myvatn also has a spectacular restaurant on the ground floor with seating for up to 120 people, giving guests the perfect opportunity to enjoy breathtaking views of the lake. What's more, there is a fantastic lake-view sauna and plenty of outdoor activities, such as horseback riding, bird watching and more - making it the ideal spot for relaxation. Exploring North Iceland has never been easier!"}]}]}

title: Swimming pool with stunning views and a hidden gem
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hofsós Sundlaug is a great public swimming pool to enjoy. It overlooks the fjord and just behind the building, walk down to see the cool basalt columns!"}]}]}

title: Unique waterfall - hidden gem
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"While driving through Kirkjubæjarklaustur, stop by for another cool waterfall! It's surprisingly powerful and you can get pretty close without a long walk."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This waterfall is far less crowded than others in the area and is just an easy 5 minute walk from the parking lot."}]}]}

title: Luxurious fragrance shop & immersive scent tour
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Fischersund is a luxurious fragrance brand based in Iceland, focused on the creative spirit of clean Icelandic beauty and the healing powers of natural herbs."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Visiting their shop is a unique and memorable experience. It's also a great place to buy souveniers for yourself or someone else."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may also book a guided scent tour for an immersive experience."}]}]}

title: Enchanting pond with ducks & geese
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tjörnin, Reykjavik's central city pond, is one of Iceland's most beloved natural features. This is a great spot for a stroll, especially with kids as they chase the ducks and geese."}]}]}

title: Popular spot for viewing the Northern Lights
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Grótta is a small island easily accessible from Reykjavik and is a popular spot to view the Northern Lights when the skies are clear and dark and the KP index is high."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Free parking is available nearby but can fill up quickly. You may also walk to the lighthouse during low tide."}]}]}

title: Harpa Concert Hall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Harpa Concert Hall and Conference Centre is a beautiful venue in Reykjavík, Iceland that offers innovative design, thrilling performances, and exciting events. This iconic destination was designed by the renowned Danish artist Olafur Eliasson and Henning Larsen Architects, and features a unique, glass façade inspired by the basalt landscape of Iceland. Guests can marvel at this stunning work of art in its full glory, or take part in one of the many concerts and performances held in the concert hall. Harpa also hosts conferences, exhibitions, and other special occasions throughout the year."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The lobby is open to the public, without a ticket and you can walk around to see any art installations they may have, eat at the restaurant inside, or do some shopping. They also have a new VR experience similar to FlyOver Iceland."}]}]}

title: Viking ship sculpture on the water
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Stop by this iconic sculpture along the harbor. This is a frequent stop for sightseeing tours."}]}]}

title: Traditional Icelandic wool goods handmade in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you want to buy authentic Icelandic Wool Sweaters this is where to go!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Handknitting Association of Iceland is a group of mostly women that have been producing handknitted goods since 1977. They specialise in crafting items such as sweaters, pullovers, cardigans and blankets from the unique wool of Icelandic sheep. All products are handmade by members of the association using traditional knitting techniques."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Be sure to ask the staff about the VAT refund, especially if you're spending quite a bit of money here."}]}]}

title: Famous Rainbow Road surrounded by shops
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Skólavörðustígur, also known as the Reykjavík Rainbow Street, is a pedestrian street in downtown Reykjavik, Iceland. It's a very popular photo spot where you can also get Hallgrimskirkja in the background."}]}]}

title: Delicious bakery
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Brauð & Co is a popular bakery that is always busy!"}]}]}

title: Majestic whale museum
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is my favorite museum in Reykjavik. You get to see the whales in real size and understand how big they actually are. They have all from Orcas to Humbacks and Blue Whale. Definitely worth a visit."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you are going on a whale watching tour, Special Tours also offers admission to this museum alongside your tour."}]}]}

title: Unexpected penis museum in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Icelandic Phallological Museum is an unconventional museum that houses the world's largest display of penises and penile parts from various species of animals."}]}]}

title: Year-round campsite at the national park
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are 4 different campgrounds here and it is open year-round. You'll need to check-in at the Thingvellir Information Centre upon arrival."}]}]}

title: Cafe near Skogafoss
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Located just inside the Skogar museum but you don't need museum tickets to eat here."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Coffee & tea, sandwiches, and pastries"}]}]}

title: Búðardalur Campsite - year round
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Búðardalur is a campsite open year round in a convenient location between Snaefellsnes Peninsula, Westfjords, and North Iceland."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It's also a good choice for tent camping because there are trees nearby to protect your tent from the wind."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The campsite also has a convenience store, coffee shop, and playground."}]}]}

title: Thousands of stones, rocks, gems, & minerals from Iceland!
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Petra's Stone Collection is a unique and enchanting garden and \"museum\" in East Iceland. Located inside Petra's home, this is a great little stop to make to break up the drive along the Eastfjords. The museum is run by Petra's family and friends and even if you're not that interested in stones, the sheer number of items in Petra's collection is astonishing."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Closed in winter."}]}]}

title: Petting zoo and horse rides
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Finnsstaðir is a family-run business located just four minutes away from the town Egilsstaðir. Aside from their guesthouse and horse rental, one of its main attractions is its Petting Zoo from May - September.
