Here guests are invited to get an up close look at some of Iceland's native animals such as lambs, calves, pigs, ducklings, rabbits and guinea pigs."}]}]}

title: Delicious, Michelin recognized seafood & wine
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"TIDES Restaurant is located inside one of Reykjavik's best hotels, The Reykjavik EDITION. It is also recognized and featured in the Michelin Guide. TIDES specializes in seafood and wine."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Serving breakfast, lunch and dinner."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reservations are highly recommended, especially for dinner and during peak season."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Other dining options available at TIDES Restaurant are:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"TIDES Counter - An interactive, 12-course dining experience for no more than 8 is available on Fridays at 6:30 pm."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"TIDES Cafe - Coffee, pastries, grab & go items"}]}]}

title: Underground paid parking - great for city exploring
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hafnartorg Car Park is centrally located in Reykjavik and will keep your rental car dry. This is a great place to park if you're exploring the city for the day. You may pay via the PARKA app or at the machine in the garage."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note: street parking is also available around the city and you'll need to pay at a pay station."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Free parking is offered on either side of and at the back of Hallgrimskirkja church."}]}]}

title: Fun cafe with a few washers and dryers
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Laundromat Cafe in Reykjavík is an upbeat and casual place to hang out, enjoy your favourite food and drinks, and even do some laundry. It's designed with classic American-diner style furniture - leather stools around the bar, full tables and booths, plus a selection of books, board games, newspapers, magazines and WiFi for extra entertainment! The menu has something for everyone, from healthy brunches to Sunday roasts, burgers, sandwiches, soups and salads. They also have an excellent selection of wines and beers--including local craft options-- and a playroom for kids. On top of that, visitors can also make use of the laundry machines in the basement."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are a good number of vegetarian and vegan choices as well."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is more of a casual place to eat than a laundromat but if you really need to get your washing done, this is a good place to keep you entertained while you wait."}]}]}

title: Iceland's only real laundromat
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is the only real laundromat you'll find in Iceland. You may also try The Laundromat Cafe in Reykjavik."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here, detergent is provided."}]}]}

title: Groovy American bar experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Lebowski Bar is a fun, retro American bar located in Reykjavík. It's inspired by Big Lebowski and serves a large variety of White Russians, burgers, milkshakes, cocktails and beer."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"They also host quiz nights and have live music every night."}]}]}

title: Cute little turf church in South-East Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hofskirkja is a sweet little turf church in South-East Iceland, and it is the last of it's kind to be built in this traditional style. Add it as a quick stop if you're looking for a break from driving."}]}]}

title: Self-service buffet style restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reasonably priced and conveniently located restaurant. If you're looking for a late breakfast or lunch near Jokulsarlon Glacier Lagoon and you'd rather not eat at the food trucks, this is a good option."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It also has a nice view of the glacier."}]}]}

title: Hotel in Höfn
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Milk Factory is another standard hotel in Höfn. A self-served continental breakfast in the common area is included with all room reservations."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note: this hotel does not have a restaurant."}]}]}

title: Christmas Market at Árbær Open Air Museum
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is one of my favourite markets as it's just next to where I grew up and I always went here as a kid. If you're not Icelandic, this market offers a unique opportunity to experience an authentic Icelandic Christmas. It stands out by providing more chances for active participation in the creation and practice of Christmas traditions. Whether it's knitting, crafting decorations, baking leaf bread, or even learning to make candles, you'll find yourself immersed in a setting reminiscent of a scene from a Christmas movie."}]}]}

title: Christmas Market at Ingolfstorg
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"What a delightful way to enjoy a chilly winter evening—either gracefully gliding across the ice or, for most, humorously clinging to the side to maintain a sense of elegance. The artificial ice rink in the square creates a festive ambiance, with wooden huts adorned with Christmas lights casting a sparkling glow. Indulge in street food delights and take in the charming surroundings."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"While not the largest Christmas market globally, Iceland's rich traditions and warm hospitality transform this compact gem into a memorable experience. Don't forget to bring your own skates for complimentary entry!"}]}]}

title: Christmas Market in Heiðmörk
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discover the magical Heiðmörk woods hosting yet another enchanting Christmas Market. Embracing a more laid-back atmosphere, this market offers a plethora of homemade treasures, ranging from products and crafts to delightful food. Only a 15-minute drive from Reykjavik, you'll find yourself immersed in this woodland wonderland, accompanied by the soothing tones of live music."}]}]}

title: Christmas Village in Hafnarfjörður
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Opening times: Every Friday between 17:00 - 20:00, Saturday and Sunday between 13:00 – 18:00 during the weeks of Advent."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Christmas Village in Hafnarfjörður, Iceland, is a festive delight for the senses, creating a seasonal wonderland. It beckons visitors with picturesque Christmas houses offering a delightful array of Icelandic design, crafts, snacks, and hot beverages. Live entertainment fills the air at the market, and you might even have the chance to encounter some of the iconic Icelandic Yule Lads."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For an ideal day at the Christmas Village, begin with a relaxing soak in one of the town's geothermal pools. Follow it up with a tour of local art galleries and museums, immersing yourself in the cultural scene. Take a stroll through Hellisgerði Park, known as the abode of the Hidden Folk, or test your skills on the newly established ice skating rink, Hjartasvellið. Conclude the day on a high note with a delectable meal, rounding off your festive experience."}]}]}

title: Campsite at Vestrahorn/Stokksnes - open year round
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"To check-in at the campsite, head to Viking Cafe."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a popular campsite and has stunning sunsets and sunrises. If you'd like to stay here during peak travel season, try to arrive earlier in the day."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"They also have a guesthouse with a few rooms with access to Stokksnes and breakfast included."}]}]}

title: Restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Galito restaurant serves a wide variety of food - burgers, sandwiches, pizza, salad, and sushi on the weekends."}]}]}

title: Unique cafe in Hafnarfjall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Cozy and funky cafe serving coffee, light meals, sandwiches, paninis, soup, deserts & vegan options."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"They also have a few budget rooms for rent with shared bathrooms and kitchen."}]}]}

title: Guesthouse & Restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Englendingavík homestay is a cozy guesthouse with an on-site restaurant."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"They have some private rooms and a few rooms with shared bathroom & kitchen."}]}]}

title: Beautiful Icelandic hexagonal basalt columns
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Gerðuberg Cliffs is a fascinating geological site located on the Snӕfellsnes Peninsula in western Iceland. This scenic area consists of perfectly shaped hexagonal basalt columns that form a stunning wall along the roadside. These unique structures were created by lava flowing from cliffs thousands of years ago, and cooled so quickly by the sea that it resulted in these remarkable shapes."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"From the parking lot you can walk along the base of the columns and then climb up the columns themselves for a view of the surrounding landscape and maybe even see the Snæfellsjökull glacier in the distance."}]}]}

title: Basic restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Stapinn is a simple restaurant with coffee drinks, burgers, fish & chips, and soup. It's a decent option along the peninsula route."}]}]}

title: Small and sweet cafe
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Fjöruhúsið café is a small little restaurant with great food and great views. Their quiche, waffles and cakes are delicious!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Only open during the summer (June through September usually)"}]}]}

title: White sand beach!
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Skarðsvík Beach is one of the few white sand beaches in Iceland and often skipped by travelers."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"On a nice and clear day you may even be able to see the Westfjords!"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Use caution when driving here - especially in the winter as the road is not maintained and can be very bumpy."}]}]}

title: Inexpensive accommodation
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Freezer Hostel, Apartments & Culture Centre park is uniquely situated on the Snaefellsnes Peninsula. This one-of-a-kind facility has a variety of accommodation types to choose from – whether it’s budget-friendly dormitory rooms with a chilled atmosphere, comfortable apartments complete with private bathrooms and fully equipped kitchenettes, or a variety of cultural events on offer throughout the year. There is a shared lounge area with games and TVs, an outdoor terrace ideal for relaxation, and even a movie theater! "}]}]}
locationName: The Freezer Hostel, Apartments & Culture Centre

title: Stunning Icelandic dining experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Bjargarsteinn Mathús restaurant is a cute restaurant with great food and an incredible view. It's a wonderful spot to enjoy dinner but their hours can be limited, and sometimes they're closed, especially in the winter. Be sure to check their website and Facebook page for information."}]}]}

title: Learn about the Greenland shark and try some fermented shark
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Bjarnarhöfn Shark Museum provides a unique educational experience about the process of the famous Icelandic fermented shark. Visitors can join a guided tour of the museum and learn about the Greenland sharks biology as well as their importance in Icelandic history. After the tour, visitors can see the drying house where the shark meat is hung and taste the shark meat inside the museum for a unique experience. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Closed in the winter."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Bistro on site as well."}]}]}

title: Delicious locally-sourced culinary adventures
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Sjávarpakkhúsið, located in Stykkishólmur within West Iceland, is a restaurant that offers stunning views of the harbour and a selection of locally sourced and seasonal food. They pride themselves on their sustainability and locality; emphasising high quality produce with a low environmental impact. The restaurant is certified with the Nordic Swan Ecolabel and serves a range of beers and wines to accompany its variety of dishes. "}]}]}

title: Delicious Icelandic cuisine - Reservations Necessary
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Narfeyrarstofa restaurant and café is the oldest operating restaurant in Stykkishólmur, Iceland and offers inventive dishes served artfully in beautiful surroundings with stunning views over Breiðafjörður bay. The chefs source all ingredients locally wherever possible."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a small restaurant with limited seating so I highly recommend making a reservation in advance."}]}]}

title: Ferry from Stykkisholmur to Brjanslaekur (Westfjords)
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A quick and efficient way to get from Snaefellsnes Peninsula to the Westfjords is to take a ferry. You may bring your rental vehicle on board the ferry but you will need to know the dimensions of your vehicle to book a ticket."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"In the summer months, you may choose to stay for the day or spend a night at Flatey Island - this will need to be arranged with the ferry company as you will not be allowed to drive your vehicle on Flatey, the ferry company will take your vehicle to Brjanslaekur and it will wait for you there. Most things are closed on Flatey outside of summer so I do not recommend spending time there from September - May."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The ferry runs year round but the schedule in winter offers far fewer departure times."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The ferry trip from Stykkisholmur to Brjanslaekur is about 2.5 hours long."}]}]}

title: Ferry from Brjansjaekur (Westfjords) to Stykkisholmur
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is the port where you'll hop off or on to get from Stykkisholumur on the Snaefellsnes Peninsula. You may also bring your vehicle aboard the ferry."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"In summer you may choose to spend some time at Flatey Island, either a few hours or overnight, but this will need to be arranged with the ferry company because they will be taking your vehicle to Stykkisholmur while you're in Flatey."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The ferry ride is about 2.5 hours long."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The ferry operates year round but with a much more limited schedule outside of summer. I do not recommend spending time in Flatey outside of summer as many things you'll need are closed."}]}]}

title: Fish & Chips, burgers & cocktails
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Good option for lunch or dinner (be sure to check their hours for lunch)."}]}]}

title: Nice hotel in Stykkisholmur
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Fransiskus Hotel in Stykkisholmur is good option for most groups and solo travelers. They have a wide variety of rooms and breakfast is always included."}]}]}

title: Fuel, charging station & mini-market
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"With so few options around this area, you may find this N1 station at the perfect time to fill up on fuel, charge your EV, or grab some food. The market here has quite a bit to choose from - grocery type items plus hot food from the kitchen."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You may also eat here at their tables."}]}]}

title: Handmade Wool Sweater Shop
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is my favourite Icelandic Wool Sweater Shop in Iceland. All sweaters are handmade by local Icelanders and they have so many designs to pick from. Each sweater is guaranteed unique and you support locals by bying from here"}]}]}

title: Campsite in Kirkjubæjarklauster - year round
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Campsite in Kirkjubæjarklauster offering a few heated cottages and all of the typical amenities, including a washer and dryer. Try to arrive for check-in between 16:00 and 21:00."}]}]}

title: Aurora Hotel Keflavik Airport
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hotel Aurora at Keflavik Airport is the perfect place to stay when in Iceland. It's only 100 meters away from Keflavik International Airport, making it extremely convenient for travellers looking for a short accommodation. With spacious rooms with private bathrooms and seating areas, plus Continental breakfast and free wifi, you can enjoy all the benefits of a comfortable stay at an affordable price. "}]}]}

title: Delicious café and bakery - great for breakfast!
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Sandholt Reykjavik is a popular café and bakery located on Laugavegur Street in Reykjavík, part of the Sands hotel. It offers a wide selection of baked goods, pastries, sandwiches, and salads. The café serves both seated dining and take-away options."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reservations are recommended but only for groups of 5 or more. If you don't have reservations, expect to wait."}]}]}

title: Michelin Distinguished & Sustainable Restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Dill Restaurant is a hip spot in Reykjavik, Iceland that offers high quality cooking using traditional Icelandic methods and modern techniques. The menu, designed by internationally experienced chefs, consists of creative, diminutive dishes that showcase sustainable ingredients and reflect the restaurant's strong sustainability ethos. Guests can choose from multi-course meals, served and explained directly by the chefs themselves. With its moody atmosphere, Dill promises a memorable culinary experience for diners of all kinds."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reservations HIGHLY recommended. Reservations open 3 months in advance."}]}]}

title: Small Cafe & Market
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The charming Old General Store (Kaupfjélagið) located in Breiðdalsvík, Iceland is where you can stop in for a cup of coffee, fish & chips or a freshly baked cake, or shop for local goods."}]}]}

title: Cozy restaurant in the Eastfjords
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Pizza, burgers, meat & seafood dishes, beer & wine."}]}]}

title: Incredible Icelandic cuisine - closed in winter
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Nielsen restaurant is located in the heart of Egilsstaðir, in one of the oldest houses in town. The owners Sólveig and Kári, a renowned chef in Iceland, strive to offer guests an authentic culinary experience of local and seasonal Icelandic ingredients. They can sit inside or when the weather permits on the tree surrounded patio. At Nielsen, every bite will be something special with access to the freshest produce and changing menus that adjust according to the season’s availability. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reservations HIGHLY recommended as opening hours are very limited."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Open from June through September."}]}]}

title: Pizzas & beer
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Askur Pizzeria & taproom is a great place to enjoy some pizza and beer. Or, order takeaway pizza! "}]}]}

title: Large campsite open year round
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Camp Egilsstadir is an great campsite located in a small town called Egilsstaðir, in the east Iceland on the banks of Lagarfljót river. It offers everything from modern facilities (including free Wi-Fi) and 24/7 services to a natural shelter under the girdle of cliffs and discounts for Vök Baths hot springs and Axe throwing. The Egilsstaðastofa Visitor Center also provides information about traveling in the Egilsstaðir area and the available services all year long. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note: during high season you'll need to reserve and pay for a camping spot online."}]}]}

title: Small museum highlighting Iceland in Eurovision
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Eurovision Museum in Húsavík, Iceland recently opened its doors with an exciting exhibition dedicated to celebrating Iceland's participation in the Eurovision Song Competition. On display are various props from Iceland's televised selection show Söngvakeppnin such as Hatari's pugil stick, Gagnamagnið's keytars, and Yohanna’s Is It True dress. Icelandic Eurovision legend Greta Salóme (2012 and 2016) also performed with the Húsavík children’s choir who sang with Molly Sandén at the 93rd Academy Awards.  "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The museum is also home to the Jaja Ding Dong Bar where you can enjoy snacks and cocktails."}]}]}

title: Nice hotel in this small town
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Located in the heart of Husavik, this hotel offers guests 44 comfortable and clean rooms fully-equipped with amenities. Enjoy views of Skjalfandi Bay from the hotel property, or take advantage of the many nearby activities."}]}]}

title: Delightful Icelandic dining
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Pizza, burgers, beef, chicken, fish & lamb"}]}]}

title: Fill up with fuel and food
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reykjahlíð is a quaint and captivating village situated on the banks of Lake Mývatn in North Iceland. Restaurant options are slim but this supermarket is a great stop to make when touring the Mývatn area for the day."}]}]}

title: Delicious Pizzas in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Daddi’s Pizza, located near Lake Myvatn in Iceland, offers delicious pizzas that are sure to satisfy any appetite. With a variety of choices including vegan options, there is something for everyone! "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Eat-in or takeaway"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Closed in winter"}]}]}

title: Restaurant and market
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Dalakofinn restaurant is a restaurant and small market near Godafoss. Their restaurant is mainly pizzas and burgers but they also have other traditional dishes as well as beer, a few wines, and some liquor."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Open year round."}]}]}

title: Almost all INTERNATIONAL flights land here
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Keflavík International Airport, also known as Reykjavík–Keflavík Airport, is the largest airport in Iceland and serves as the main hub for international travel. Located about 50 km southwest of Reykjavík, it's a bustling gateway for travelers coming to and from North America and Europe. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There is also a DOMESTIC airport in Reykjavik city centre (RKV) - be careful not to confuse the two."}]}]}

title: Drangsnes Hot Tubs
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Drangsnes Hot Tubs is a hidden gem in the small fishing town of Drangsnes, Iceland. Tucked away right on the shoreline, these three hot tubs offer a unique experience for visitors. The best part? They're fed by natural geothermal vents, so you can soak in the warm waters while enjoying the stunning views of the sea. Plus, there's even a cool pool, a hot one, and a medium one, so you can find the perfect temperature to relax in. And if you're lucky, you might even catch a glimpse of the Northern Lights during the winter months. So grab a beer from the honor bar, kick back, and let the soothing waters wash your worries away."}]}]}

title: Áfangagil campsite
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Áfangagil Park is a hidden gem in Iceland, known for its stunning landscapes and unique geological features. Visitors can explore lava fields, towering cliffs, and even catch a glimpse of some local wildlife. Whether you're into hiking, birdwatching, or just soaking in the natural beauty, Áfangagil Park has something for everyone. So grab your camera and get ready to be wowed by this epic Icelandic destination!"}]}]}

title: Scenic and cozy camping spot
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Bíldudalur Camping Ground offers a cozy spot for travelers to pitch their tents or park their campers in the picturesque village of Bíldudalur. With amenities like showers, electricity, and a playground, it's a great place to unwind after a day of exploring the stunning Westfjords. Plus, you can enjoy a budget-friendly stay with rates starting at 1,775 ISK for adults. So, if you're looking for a laid-back camping experience surrounded by nature, Bíldudalur Camping Ground has got you covered!"}]}]}

title: Experience real lava up close
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"LAVA SHOW is the only live lava show in the world and has received multiple innovation awards and recognition for its educational and cultural value. With outstanding customer reviews, Lava Show is one of the highest rated attractions in Iceland and an utterly unique immersive experience unlike anything else in the world. Don’t miss this unique opportunity to get up close to real molten lava, see it flowing, hear it sizzling, and feel the incredible heat that emanates from it. Edutainment at its finest!"}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"There are two Lava Show locations - Reykjavik and Vik on the south coast. Both provide the key attraction of flowing lava but they each have a different storytelling component."}]}]}

title: Cozy café with stunning views
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Stúkuhúsið Café/Restaurant is a cozy spot in Patreksfjörður, nestled right next to the pool with killer views of the fjord. They dish up homemade soups, freshly baked bread, scrumptious cakes, and local goodies. It's the perfect pit stop for a tasty bite and a caffeine fix while soaking in the stunning surroundings. Check out their Facebook page or website for the scoop on their opening hours and menu!"}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Closed in winter."}]}]}

title: Vík Lupine Fields
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Above the church in Vik you'll find a lupine field that is great for photography, overlooking the church, ocean and sea stacks"}]}]}

title: Hidden gem waterfall in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Raudarfoss waterfall, also known as Rauðárfoss in Icelandic, is a hidden gem in South Iceland that's totally worth checking out. Standing at around 66 feet tall, this majestic waterfall gets its name from the red water flowing down its cascading streams, thanks to the iron-rich soil in the riverbed. Tucked away from the crowds, Raudarfoss offers a breathtaking sight with its veil-like flow, making it a must-see stop on your Icelandic adventure. Just a short hike from the mountain road, Nyrdri Fjallabak, this off-the-beaten-path beauty is waiting to wow you with its unique charm."}]}]}

title: Red eye river
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Rauðauga park in Iceland is a hidden gem that will give you serious Lord of the Rings vibes. With its stunning red waterfalls and crystal-clear pool at Rauðauga.  It's about a 2h return hike from the parking spot. The red waterfall is halfway and from there you can choose to follow the red river up to it's source, the red eye. It looks really good both on drone and camera from the ground."}]}]}

title: Iconic green cone volcano hike
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Maelifell volcano in Iceland is a stunning green cone-shaped mountain located in the remote highlands. Covered in vibrant moss, it stands out against the black sands of Maelifellsandur and is a popular filming location for movies and TV shows. Accessible via challenging F-roads, visitors can drive or join a tour to reach this iconic sight. The hike to the colourful Rauðibotn crater offers breathtaking views of the surrounding area, making it a must-do adventure in Iceland."}]}]}

title: Stunning waterfall in Southeast Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Fagrifoss, also known as the Beautiful Waterfall, is a stunning waterfall located in Southeast Iceland near the Lakagígar volcanic region. Its smooth brink and segmented plunge create a beautiful fan shape at its base, making it a picturesque sight against the raw landscapes surrounding it. To reach Fagrifoss, travelers must cross a small river, so a 4x4 vehicle is needed for the crossing. It's definitely worth a visit if you're exploring the area on a self-drive tour or looking to experience Iceland's majestic waterfalls."}]}]}

title: Skaftárhreppur Waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Skaftárhreppur Waterfall is easily accesible just next to the road. Perfect for a quick stop when driving into Laki area"}]}]}

title: Mountain lodge in Icelandic wilderness
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hungurfit in Rangárvallaafréttur offers cozy lodge facilities nestled in the stunning Icelandic wilderness. With a history dating back to 1963, this mountain retreat has come a long way from its humble beginnings. The modern lodge can accommodate up to 50 people and boasts amenities like running water, flushable toilets, and electricity. Perfect for outdoor enthusiasts, Hungurfit is an ideal base for hiking, jeeping, and horse riding adventures. Don't miss the chance to explore the unique natural beauty of this area and soak in the tranquility of the Icelandic landscape."}]}]}

title: Laki Craters
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"On your way to Tjarnargigar, you'll find a row of old craters. They look very cool from a drone perspective"}]}]}

title: Impressive multi-drop waterfall in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Ófærufoss waterfall in Iceland is a stunning 40-meter tall multi-drop waterfall located in the Icelandic Highlands. Its name, meaning 'the impassable waterfall,' perfectly captures its rugged beauty and remote location. The waterfall flows into the Eldgjá Canyon. While access to Ófærufoss may require a bit of hiking and a sturdy 4x4 vehicle, the breathtaking views and unique geological features make it well worth the effort."}]}]}

title: Básar Hut & campsite
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Básar Hut and campsite is a cozy spot nestled in the Thorsmörk valley. There's some snalle rriver crossings on the way in and it's accesible by most 4x4. You don't need to cross the deep Krossá which makes Básar the perfect stop. From Básar you can reach many great hikes in the amazing thorsmörk valley"}]}]}

title: Lonely mountain photo spot
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Krakatindur is a badass volcano in the Icelandic Highlands that hasn't erupted since 1878. It's a perfect photo spot on the road between Rauðaskál and Raudufossar waterfall"}]}]}

title: Hidden fairyland in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Gjáin is a hidden gem tucked away in Þjórsárdalur valley in South-Iceland and very popular by locals on a sunny summer day.  You can hike to the area from the Viking settlement manor at Stöng, or drive up the road to the main parking lot. Note that this road crosses a small river and is very bumpy. From the parking lot, it's a 15 min hike to get down the canyon and to the waterfalls."}]}]}

title: Spacious oceanfront villa for groups
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Luxury Ocean Villa is a spacious and stylish house on Heimaey in the Westman Islands, perfect for families and large groups. With four bedrooms accommodating up to ten guests, it offers modern amenities, stunning ocean views, and a geothermally heated hot tub for relaxation."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The best part about the villas is the view, looking directly at the Elephant rock!"}]}]}

title: Volcanic eruption on Heimaey Island
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Eldfell is a volcanic cone on the Icelandic island of Heimaey that popped up unexpectedly in 1973, causing chaos for the locals. Today you can hike to the top of the volcano and get a great view of all of the Westman Islands. The hike is relatively short and will take you 20-30min to get to the top."}]}]}

title: Cozy bakery with stunning views
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vigtin Bakhús in Vestmannaeyjar is a cozy bakery and cafe with a great selection of pastries, sandwiches, and coffee. Located on the harbor front, it offers a perfect spot to relax and enjoy some delicious treats while watching the hustle and bustle of the harbor. The staff is friendly, the atmosphere is welcoming, and the views are simply stunning. It's a must-visit for anyone looking for a tasty bite in a charming setting on Heimaey Island."}]}]}

title: Gourmet Icelandic cuisine in Reykjavík
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Fish Company is a top-notch restaurant located in Reykjavík, Iceland, known for its gourmet cuisine that combines the best of land and sea ingredients. With a cozy atmosphere in a 19th-century building, it's the perfect spot for a fine dining experience. Owned by Masterchef Lárus Gunnar Jónasson, the restaurant offers a unique culinary journey inspired by Icelandic nature. Whether you're a foodie looking for a special gift or craving a five-course meal, The Fish Company has got you covered with their delicious offerings."}]}]}

title: Greenhouse dining with Icelandic flavors
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Sól Restaurant is a brand new concept and a must try when you're in Reykjavik. The restaurant is located in side a large greenhouse where they grow their own produce. The dining area is directly above the growing area, only separated by a glass floor. In combination with great food this is really a unique dining experience. "}]}]}

title: Whale-watching tours in Akureyri
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Special Tours Akureyri offers exhilarating whale-watching tours in the scenic fjord of Eyjafjörður, where visitors can encounter humpback whales and other cetacean species in their natural habitat. Operating from May to September with daily departures, these tours provide a unique opportunity to connect with North Iceland's wildlife and landscape. With knowledgeable guides and a comfortable catamaran, Lilja, it's the perfect way to experience the ocean's gentle giants up close and personal."}]}]}

title: Cozy cottages & horse riding adventures
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hestasport Cottages and Riding Tours in Varmahlíð offer a cozy retreat with seven timber cottages overlooking the stunning Skagafjörður valley. From studio-style bungalows to spacious multi-room options, these cottages provide a perfect base for exploring Northern Iceland. Guests can unwind in the natural hot tub, enjoy the midnight sun or catch a glimpse of the northern lights. With a range of riding tours available, visitors can also experience the unique Icelandic horse in the picturesque surroundings of Skagafjörður. Whether you're seeking relaxation or adventure, Hestasport has something for everyone year-round."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Use code "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"ASASTEINARS"},{"type":"text","text":" for 10% off "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://riding.is/riding/#short","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":"text-title underline"}}],"text":"Horseback Riding Tours"},{"type":"text","text":"!"}]}]}

title: Coffee shop in downtown Reykjavik.
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Kaffi Ó-le Cafe is a cozy and inviting coffee shop located in the heart of Reykjavik, Iceland. This funky little cafe serves up delicious brews and tasty pastries in a vibrant and colorful atmosphere that's sure to put a smile on your face. Whether you're looking for a quick pick-me-up or a relaxing spot to unwind with friends, Kaffi Ó-le Cafe has got you covered. With friendly baristas, funky decor, and a great selection of drinks and snacks, this cafe is the perfect place to soak up the local vibe and enjoy a bit of Icelandic charm."}]}]}

title: Icelandic horseback riding
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Ride through the flat land along hills and mountains nearby Skálakot to the Írafoss waterfall. "}]}]}

title: Grocery store
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Kronan is the best supermarket in Iceland. This one is perfectly located in Selfoss. I recommend stopping here to pick up food for at least a 2-3 days if you are traveling by campervan."}]}]}

title: Indulgent Donuts & Bagel Sandwiches
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"DEIG workshop is a popular bakery located in Reykjavík.. Known for their delicious bagels and mouthwatering desserts, this place is a must."}]}]}

title: Cozy gastropub with delicious food
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Sæta Svínið Gastropub is a cozy and lively spot in Reykjavík, known for its delicious food and great drinks. With a menu featuring everything from tasty pub grub to gourmet dishes, there's something for everyone to enjoy. The atmosphere is welcoming and perfect for hanging out with friends or grabbing a bite to eat. Plus, they often host fun events like karaoke nights and Sunday roasts, adding to the overall vibe of this popular gastropub. So, whether you're looking for a casual meal or a night out on the town, Sæta Svínið is definitely worth checking out."}]}]}

title: Delicious ramen in cozy atmosphere
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Ramen Momo is a cozy little spot that serves up delicious bowls of ramen in the heart of the city. With a menu full of flavorful broths, fresh noodles, and tasty toppings, it's the perfect place to warm up on a chilly day or satisfy your noodle cravings. Plus, the friendly staff and laid-back atmosphere make it a great spot to hang out with friends while slurping down some tasty ramen."}]}]}

title: Cozy spot with local produce
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Apotek kitchen+ bar is a new and exciting restaurant located in one of the most beautiful corners of Reykjavik. The restaurant is casual-smart, offering delicious food in vibrant atmosphere and stylish surroundings. The menu is a fun mix of Icelandic and European cuisine with a smoking hot Argentinean grill. Dishes are designed to share and enjoy together."}]}]}

title: Vibrant dining with diverse options
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Pósthús Food Hall & Bar in Reykjavík is not your average food hall—it's a vibrant hub of culinary diversity and social buzz. With a mix of sushi, funky Bhangra flavors, contemporary Italian pizza, cocktails, French bistro dishes, Korean street food, and more, there's something for every palate. Whether you're looking for a quick bite or a leisurely meal, Pósthús has got you covered with its inviting atmosphere and delicious offerings. Don't forget to check out their electronic gift cards for the perfect foodie present!"}]}]}

title: Cozy family-owned coffee house
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Grái Kötturinn, also known as The Grey Cat, is a cozy family-owned coffee house in Iceland that has been serving up delicious breakfast and lunch since 1997. With a menu boasting homemade bread, pancakes, tuna salad, and more, this spot is a must-visit for coffee lovers and foodies alike. "}]}]}

title: Affordable and diverse street food
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"101 Reykjavik Street Food is a popular food truck in Reykjavik, Iceland that serves up delicious and affordable street food. From traditional Icelandic dishes like lamb soup and fish stew to international favorites like tacos and burgers, there's something for everyone to enjoy. The laid-back atmosphere and friendly staff make it a great spot to grab a quick bite while exploring the city."}]}]}

title: Fusion Nikkei cuisine in Reykjavik
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Monkeys Reykjavik is a cozy and vibrant restaurant located in the heart of Hjartagarður. They specialize in Nikkei cuisine, which combines Japanese and Peruvian flavors to create exciting small dishes that will tantalize your taste buds. With an emphasis on fresh ingredients and a diverse wine selection, Monkeys offers a unique dining experience that blends the best of both worlds. Whether you're a foodie looking to explore new flavors or just craving a delicious meal, Monkeys Reykjavik has something for everyone to enjoy."}]}]}

title: Icelandic Restaurant
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Laid-back, family-run eatery offering traditional dishes from locally sourced produce."}]}]}

title: Restaurant in downtown Reykjavik
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Le KocK makes has a variety of food options, delicious bagels, burgers, wings and delicious baked pastries. They cook everything in house and use Icelandic ingredients wherever possible."}]}]}

title: Cozy Icelandic cuisine in Reykjavik
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reykjavik Kitchen is a cozy and welcoming restaurant in Reykjavik, Iceland, known for its delicious Icelandic cuisine and friendly atmosphere. With a menu featuring traditional dishes like lamb stew and fish of the day, this spot is perfect for those looking to experience authentic Icelandic flavors in a relaxed setting. Whether you're a local or a visitor, Reykjavik Kitchen is sure to satisfy your cravings for hearty and tasty food."}]}]}

title: Relaxing oasis in Reykjavik
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is my favorite pool in Iceland to go to. This is a popular swimming pool in Reykjavík. You can find a geothermally heated outdoor pool, hot tubs, a cold bath, steam room, a play pool for kids, saunas, and even a water slide. "}]}]}

title: Remote hot springs with fjord views
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hidden in the Westfjords, Pollurinn Hot Springs offer a unique and free thermal bath experience. With three small pools heated by natural springs, visitors can relax in water temperatures ranging from 36 to 40°C while taking in stunning views of Tálknafjörður Fjord. Basic changing facilities are available, and guests must shower before entering the pools as per Icelandic tradition. Accessible only by car, this off-the-beaten-path gem is a must-visit for those seeking a tranquil and budget-friendly soak in Iceland's natural beauty."}]}]}

title: Mirrored houses in stunning Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"ÖÖD Hekla Horizon offers unique mirrored houses in South Iceland, blending seamlessly into the stunning landscape with views of mountains, lava fields, and the Hekla Volcano. From cozy retreats for couples to spacious options for families, each house provides modern comforts and access to the Northern Lights Lounge with a shared hot tub and sauna. Guests can relax in private hot tubs, enjoy the outdoor sauna, and soak in the beauty of Iceland under the open sky. With tours led by local expert Ingi Thor, visitors can explore hidden gems and experience Iceland in a way that's off the beaten path."}]}]}

title: Private Day Tour
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Go on an adventure for a day into the Icelandic Highlands. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Our guide will pick you up at your accommodation in the morning and take you on a tour into the wild Icelandic nature. Expect a full day on the road and explore off the beaten path locations in Iceland. "},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","text":"We will cater the day to your needs and the weather of the day to give you the best experience. Our guide is also a photographer and will take pictures of you during the day if you want to."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Price is $1690 including 4x4 Jeep, fuel and photography. Food is not included."}]}]}

title: Stunning Lupine Field with ocean backdrop
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is one of the most stunning lupine fields of Iceland. It's one of few that has both ocean view and mountain backdrop which makes it very unique and perfect for pictures. "}]}]}

title: Lupine filed photospot
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is my favourite photo spot in Vestmannayjar. In June when the lupines bloom you can use them as foreground for really nice images. Try to come in the evening when the sun is low. Around 9pm is the best light"}]}]}

title: Replica Norwegian church in Iceland
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Heimaey Stave Church, also known as Stafkirkjan, is a replica of the Haltdalen stave church located in Vestmannaeyjar, Iceland. Completed in 2000 as a gift from Norway to Iceland, this church was built to commemorate the millennial anniversary of Iceland's conversion to Christianity. Constructed using materials from various regions of Norway, including timber, shingles, and wrought iron, the church stands as a beautiful tribute to Icelandic history and culture."}]}]}

title: Cozy bakery with volcano view
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Faxi Bakery in South Iceland is a cozy spot with a killer view of Eyjafjallajökull volcano. They whip up fresh cinnamon rolls every hour and serve up tasty sandwiches, bagels, cookies, cakes, and espresso drinks. Don't miss their \"happy marriage cake\" made from a secret family recipe that's been passed down through the generations. It's a must-visit for a quick coffee fix or a sweet treat with a side of Icelandic charm."}]}]}

title: Icelandic Wool for kids
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I get so many questions about Atlas’s traditional Icelandic wool sweaters. It’s the warmest thing you can wear in Iceland and honestly it’s been our secret to the outdoors for generations. Even when it rains these type of sweaters push the water/moisture away."},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","text":"Emil & Lina is the perfect store to find wool sweaters for your kids bring home for friends and family. They are hand knitted in Iceland and are made of a softer wool blend that kids like. "},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","text":"Kids love them and they loos so good!"}]}]}

title: Standard Hotel with modern amenities
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This three-star hotel offers a range of accommodation options, from cozy single rooms to spacious family rooms with beautiful views. With a restaurant serving delicious local food, free outside hot tubs for relaxation, and the chance to see the Northern Lights, your stay at Hotel Laugarbakki promises to be both comfortable and exciting. "}]}]}

title: Cozy South Coast Cabin
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is a super cozy little Viking cabin just outside of Hvollsvöllur. It's nice and fresh on the inside. There's a basic kitchen and space for 4ppl to sleep. Nothing fancy but all you really need. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The best part is how it looks on the outside and the sauna. Ari is also planning to add a hot tub. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you're looking for a cozy, yet not too expensive place close to the waterfalls on the south coast then this VIking hut is perfect"}]}]}

title: Loneliest house in the world
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This tiny white house sits all alone on an island… and it’s often called the loneliest house in the world. "},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","text":"⁠"},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","text":"No roads, no lights, no other buildings…just steep cliffs, endless ocean, and puffins as your only neighbours. 🐧"},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","text":"⁠"},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","text":"But getting there isn’t easy… the cliffs are so steep that you need to be winched up or hike with climbing gear. It’s remote and wild…just like so many corners of Iceland that most people never get to see."}]}]}
